text:
Why have I chosen to hold it long term instead of selling it at a local top?

1. Because the long term thesis is very strong. 

$SIVE is involved in some of the most important verticals for the new economy. Namely

- photonics (ai)
- lidar (robotics)
- wearable sensors
- RF (drones, space tech, 5g, 6g)

They already have customers for all this. They already have partnerships with the foundries.

2. Because we already have institutional validation and ownership. $JPM and Fidelity were already revealed in the fillings. 

Then the last fundraiser was oversubscribed by multiples from institutions. 

3. When you look at where other players in photonics alone are - you have $LITE at 50-70b $AAOI at 10b $COHR at 65b. 

Then there are also the RF players, the space players, the drones players, the Lidar players. 

Sivers is in all that. So obviously if they execute their valuation will be going towards 10b and beyond.

Sure it will take time, but if the institutions are buying in, that's enough validation for me. They make calculated moves and don't panic sell like retail dilettantes.

text:
Whenever $SIVE drops and you think of pressing that “sell” button, read this post.

Look…

Markets go through waves - up and down. Simple as that.

We are still early in photonics compared to other AI cycles. Volatility is expected and will continue.

It’s the nature of early-stage cycles and high-growth tech companies like $SIVE. This isn’t the stable phase yet.

At the end of the day, it comes down to conviction in your thesis and staying unmoved through the storm if you believe it will play out.

What I can say is…

Every time I look at $SIVE, it seems to be getting better, not worse.

What’s moving the price is macro uncertainty and fear, not anything negative about the company’s performance or progress.

In fact, they are taking the right steps forward. The day they stop, I’ll stop talking about them and sell. I promise.

After all the research and analysis over the last few months, I’m very bullish and convinced on $SIVE. Building your own conviction matters. Nothing has changed for me.

Holding all shares and adding more soon. So many times I wished I could have bought more below 50 SEK. Now it’s here. 👀

Let me present that to you all.

The Goldman Sachs report has a lot of nectar in it. Beyond the overall TAM expansion numbers for optical networks & CPO, a 10x TAM jump in just two years - from $15B to $154B - is no joke. CPO alone contributes $91B of that (59%). That’s already massive. 

But let’s dig in deeper...

Mass-volume production and mainstream adoption of connection speeds beyond 1.6T+ kicks off in H2 2026 and runs through H2 2028. Overall, CPO TAM totals ~$97B from 2026-2028. $JBL, a confirmed partner with $SIVE for their 1.6T programs, already gives them solid exposure on the transceiver side. Other partners are mentioned later on.

Optics is clearly preferred over copper for higher speeds, but the costs are higher too because the transceivers and lasers needed at this scale are expensive. That means more revenue for $SIVE - because $SIVE makes the CW lasers, which are the most widely adopted light source across optical modules for both scale-out and scale-up. You can see why a moat is building here. 

On top of that, there’s supply tightness for these lasers, which adds pricing power and improves margins. Goldman Sachs expects this tightness to continue through 2027, with possible relief only in H2 2028.

This isn’t the only bullish report. 

Bank of America also published their TAM projections for Optical Networking, reaching $87.7B by 2030 at a 40% CAGR. While it looks less aggressive, that’s mainly because BofA focuses more on the direct market for optical hardware components rather than the entire networking architecture.

$SIVE’s current opportunity pipeline sits at $453M, up 64% YoY. They did $31.21M in FY2025 revenue (almost 10% of the pipeline). 

Let’s assume they keep growing the opportunity pipeline roughly 2x by 2030. I say this because the TAM is expanding 10x, and everything lines up in their favor - from supply chain constraints & Tier-1 links to CW lasers being the most widely adopted.

That gives us roughly an ~$8B opportunity pipeline. At a conservative 12.5% execution rate into revenue, that’s $1B in revenue by 2030. At 40x P/S, you’re looking at a $40B company.

And $1B isn’t out of reach, because multiple revenue streams will come in beyond just photonics:

> Biosensing through $AAPL watches could bring in almost $130M in annual revenue alone at high-volume scale.

> SatCOM & defense is another big watchout, especially after the MOU signed. With the satellite market projected to grow 3-4x by 2030 and defense spending rising to adopt new tech (backed by governments), $SIVE is well positioned. Geopolitical tensions and catalysts like a potential SpaceX IPO only add fuel.

> Photonics will clearly make up the majority of revenue (well over 50%), but this diversification derisks the business and avoids over-reliance on just a few big orders.

Now the question is: how can such a small company become so big in a few years?

> A US listing will bring much more liquidity and institutional flow. It lets them expand capacity and go directly to Tier-1 customers instead of relying on indirect channels. They’ve already hired M&A specialists to possibly being able to scale beyond just being a laser supplier - producing their own transceivers, ELS modules, and other optical components, and acquiring new IP. This expands revenue per unit and their TAM. $MRVL is did the exact same thing with Celestial AI. Revenue in 2029 solely from that division is expected to bring them ~$1B.

> Geopolitical advantage isn’t being fully priced in yet. The US wants to reduce dependency on China and Asia for manufacturing. $SIVE, as a Swedish company building a stronger US base, is perfectly placed to ride the “American Dream” vision. The MOU confirms this. Presence in India also helps, as it’s becoming a semiconductor hub (see $MU’s recent fab).

> Never underestimate retail backing. Look at what it did for $PLTR and $HOOD. I see the same thing happening here - it brings validation, higher volumes and momentum. Marketing still matters, and retail is powerful for that.

So...

The company is doing all the right things. They’re executing in a TAM that’s clearly stacked in their favor to become the next $LITE, and they have a huge, growing retail fanbase behind them.

The vision is clear and ambitious, as the CEO himself said: to become “a very successful semiconductor company by 2030, recognized the same way some of the biggest companies out there are.”

Risks exist - the story might not play out perfectly. But even then, today’s ~$2B market cap still offers 10x potential by 2030. The real legacy upside sits at 15-20x.

After reading this, I don’t know what else the AI bubble and delusional bears need to believe.

Next time before claiming something, it would be wiser to some research. 😌

text:
View on sector. $Sive 

This is the key finding: it’s sector-wide, not SIVE-specific.
SECTOR CONTEXT (this changes the read materially)
AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics): dropped roughly 17% on July 2, with Coherent and Lumentum sinking around 10% in the same session — headlines describe this as a broad “photonics stocks reset.” A separate report also flags AAOI shares as sharply lower amid a “risk-off stretch for growth” and investors “rotating out of unprofitable tech.”
XFAB (X-FAB): search results are contradictory/stale across sources (one shows +47% “last session,” another shows -8.6% weekly, dates ranging from May to July) — data quality here isn’t reliable enough for me to give you a clean today-vs-yesterday read. I’d check this one directly on your IBKR/TradingView feed.

SIVE: closed 49 SEK, down from 60.75 on July 1 (which was itself already -3.8% that day, off a 63.15 prior close). So the stock has fallen roughly three consecutive sessions since the emission.

WHAT THIS MEANS FOR YOUR READ
The AAOI/Coherent/Lumentum move on the same days tells you something important: the CPO/photonics complex broadly de-rated this week, likely tied to a “risk-off” rotation out of high-multiple, unprofitable AI-infrastructure names — not a SIVE-specific loss of confidence.

That reframes your situation:
•Sector-wide component: growth/photonics multiple compression hitting AAOI, COHR, LITE simultaneously — this is macro/rotation, not fundamental to SIVE.
•SIVE-specific component: still real — the stock is trading below its own 700 MSEK emission price, which is a distinct, company-specific overhang (institutions underwater on their entry, no fresh lock-up on management past July 16).

Both things are true at once: broad photonics de-rating plus SIVE-specific dilution digestion. That’s a worse combination than either alone, but it does mean you shouldn’t read the SIVE drop as pure company-specific panic.

text:
$Sive break $50 critical level or psychology level. —  Today it closed at 49 SEK is now 14% below the emission price of 57 SEK, not just a brief dip below it. That changes the picture materially.

WHAT THIS TELLS YOU
1. The oversubscription didn’t hold as a floor. Institutions who paid 57 SEK are already underwater too. If the “smart money” validation thesis was going to show up as support, it should have shown up in the first 48 hours. It didn’t.

2. This is now a confidence problem, not just a dilution problem. A stock breaking meaningfully below its own primary offering price within days signals the market isn’t just digesting dilution — it’s questioning the raise itself, or de-risking ahead of something (Q2 earnings, sector rotation, general AI-infrastructure profit-taking).

3. Your position math: Entry 104 → 63 (late June) → now 49. That’s roughly a 53% drawdown from cost basis. This is no longer “a drawdown to manage” — it’s a thesis-under-real-stress situation.

WHAT TO WATCH NEXT, NOT SPECULATE ON
•Volume on the break below 57: heavy volume = capitulation/distribution; light volume = could be thin summer liquidity exaggerating the move.
•Sector context: check whether XFAB, AAOI, and other photonics/CPO peers are also down today — if it’s sector-wide, this is rotation, not SIVE-specific.
•Insider behavior into July 16: management lock-up expires then. Any signs of selling pressure building ahead of that date would be a real red flag on top of this.
•Whether 57 SEK flips from floor to resistance on any bounce attempt — that’s the technical tell for whether institutions are defending their entry or already cutting losses.
Emotionally and psychologically break point #Sive

text:
Couldn’t help sharing these words by $IONQ CEO:

“The science phase of our company was done. We’re now focused on engineering and manufacturing.”

$IONQ is just an example that makes it clear quantum companies will find a way to make logical qubits.

The challenge is scaling them up. This is what $IONQ has been up to:

1/ It acquired Oxford Ionics for electronic qubit control.

2/ It acquired Lightsynq to integrate photonic interconnects to link multiple quantum units.

3/ It is going to acquire Skywater to reduce the chip design cycle from 9 months to 2 months and gain control over its supply chain.

$IONQ has a clear roadmap toward building commercially useful quantum computers.
embedded_text:
Quantum is like a freight train coming after anyone who is not invested in hybrid classical and quantum workflows.

Hear more from our CEO @NiccoloDeMasi in a newly released interview from @FII_Institute1: https://t.co/cmnmSIGDMz

text:
$sive ⚡️

Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) is a deep-tech company that designs and manufactures specialized microchips and lasers for ultra-fast, high-capacity communications.

Instead of using traditional electrical copper wiring—which creates heat and becomes a bottleneck at high speeds—Sivers builds high-power lasers and laser arrays that transmit data using light (silicon photonics).

While Photonics is the AI growth engine Sivers also designs radio frequency integrated circuits (RFICs) and beamforming antennas that operate on millimeter-wave (mmWave) frequencies.

Under 2b Market Cap this is a high risk and highly speculative play.  Current revenues/contracts and assest in no way make the current valuation.  Much of the valuation is still derived from a "potential pipeline" and "potential opportunity" but the potential upside is immense if they can execute.

$sive has run from 3sek to 100sek 🧐 
Wowsers, thats a 3000+% parabolic run.  
The current pullback is a natural part of the stock cycle and stocks that make these parabolic runs typically will retrace to .618 or 45sek or even more frequently .786 25sek.  

I missed the last run, as it had passed my buy targets and I NEVER chase but keen to enter here if she plays ball.
My intention is to scale in from 45sek to 30sek as this reduces risk and creates huge upside opportunity.

Below is my Framework score.

Sivers Semiconductors (SIVE / SIVEF)
Score: 73/100

Management & Vision  11/15
Credentialed CEO (Vathulya — NXP/Maxim/Nuvotronics, bought stock) + commercialization-weighted board refresh (Nideborn CFO, Svancar M&A, Thomson, Raj); chairman Bastani's GF network drove the GlobalFoundries deal. Capped by broad insider selling (~SEK 117M net, 12mo) + open Swedish probe, moves to 12/13 on confirmation of no wrongdoing.

Moat  12/15
Confirmed InP substrate scarcity (industry chokepoint, 6-inch pricing +250%); genuinely hard manufacturing; Glasgow fab (2M+ tests/mo, etched-facet); GF reference design + Ayar Labs supply (OCI/NVIDIA). Capped because "at scale" is still prospective. Moves to 14 on proof of scale.

TAM 14/15
InP/CPO/NPO demand structurally huge; two-division (Photonics + Wireless) exposure; GFS silicon photonics ~$25B by 2030. Unchanged.

Probable Upside  10/13
BU ~195%. Targets: 2026 SEK 70 (+4.5%), 2028 SEK 150 (+124%), 2030 SEK 320 (+377%). Weighted to 2030 execution. Targets self-built, not sell-side. Moves to 13 on contracts and proof of scale.

Revenue Quality  5/12
Confirmed facts : auditor-driven restatement widened losses, going-concern consideration, Q1 revenue -22% YoY, adj. EBITDA -13.8M SEK. Can move up fast on a clean Aug 6 print.

Conviction Gap  8/10
Market pricing max uncertainty; JPMorgan 5.25% entry post-controversy; options listing (Jul 6) unlocks institutional access. 

Risk-Adjusted  4/10
Forced/dilutive raise floating (53.8M mandate, ~15%), ~230% annualized vol, convertible at ~10.85%, thin cash (~SEK 26.6M vs -49.2M quarterly burn). Absorbs Down+TA.

Geo-Political  9/10
US/Sweden/UK — allied, stable, NDAA-friendly, no China domicile. Capped only by live Swedish regulatory probe.

text:
Serenity points out that no one should pay attention to technical analysis charts

Considering them nothing more than simple lines drawn to persuade others to sell

They highlight that $SIVE secured $70M in urgent financing to boost high volume production and lock in foundry capacity

Right at the same time companies like $JBL $POET and $AEVA are preparing short term volume ramps

They show their highest conviction in $SIVE as a long position in the photonics sector, with further production ramps from $GFS Ayar, and others lined up for 2027

Additionally, the confirmed intention to list on NASDAQ in the coming quarters shows that everything is aligning favorably

You wanted to buy $SIVE at a "discount" at 90 SEK, but you’re scared to buy the exact same company at 50 SEK

Think twice, long $SIVE

text:
People forget that just as $SIVE has dropped 50% in a matter of days, it can easily pump 200% in a few months

And I firmly believe that will happen as long as the thesis is allowed to play out

Fortunately, the money I invest is capital I don't depend on

When I was sitting on 200% returns, I didn't touch a single euro, and now that I'm 15% down, I'm still not touching anything

That’s what allows me to just hold through it

It's undeniable that seeing the portfolio drop this much hurts

And it's undeniable that at least half of the drawdown I've taken could have been avoided; it was due to my own mistakes

Mistakes from which I have learned, a lot

That’s why I’m not worried

AI isn't going anywhere

Memory, like $MU ,is here to stay, especially with a shortage projected for years

And photonics, like $SIVE, isn't dead, no matter how much you see all of FinX claiming it is

It’s very easy to talk like that right now because everything is crashing hard, but where were those posts when everything was ripping 10% daily?

Literally nothing has changed; it’s just overstimulated people rotating like headless chickens, bouncing between sectors because they don't understand a thing

It's only a matter of time before that money flows back twice as hard as it left

As soon as the sector leaders report earnings or clarify timelines regarding certain technologies, everything will bounce back

I am absolutely sure of it

Long $MU and $SIVE

text:
Serenity criticized the sharp drop in semiconductor stocks following a Bloomberg report about potential AI capex cuts by $META 

A UBS analyst pointed out that this isn't even new information, adding that $META wouldn't be signing neocloud contracts worth over $48B if they were truly scaling back capacity

They compared this overreaction to previous misleading Bloomberg reports, calling the market "stupid" for selling off so irrationally

I completely agree, this is a completely unjustified sell off

I know I might not be back at all-time highs in a month or two, but I should at least bounce back to halfway from where I was

The main issue is that I’m running out of cash, and I’m right on the edge of losses, which honestly makes it tough

But I try to remind myself that the thesis just needs time to play out

Even if I dip into the red right now, if my price targets for $MU and $SIVE are met, I could still end the year with 200% to 400% returns

It’s just a matter of time

There is no bubble here, and this has absolutely nothing to do with the dot-com crash

There are insane, massive earnings backing it all up, from $MU to $NVDA

On the other hand, $SIVE needed a correction

I admit I got greedy and never took profits

But you have to realize that everything remains the same, or even better, than it was at 4 SEK

Photonics isn't dead, even if it feels like the end of the world looking at semiconductor stocks today and throughout this week

There's nothing left to do but hold

My conviction is worth more than my current returns

Long $SIVE and $MU

I'm coming, slowly but surely! 

ForgeChip (Aether Forge Singularity Core) is a sovereign, self-reinforcing topological quantum resilience system. It is designed as a hybrid photonic-topological platform that converts adversarial inputs into system-strengthening surplus. The architecture integrates Thin-Film Lithium Niobate (TFLN) photonics, Fibonacci String-Net Condensate (Fib SNC) braiding, advanced hardware security primitives, and adaptive intelligence. It is anchored by a Personal Physiological Anchor (PPA) for human sovereignty. The project has matured through conceptual design, extensive simulations, parametric hardware layouts, patent drafting, and proposal materials. It targets dual-use applications with strong alignment to defense priorities. 

Core Architecture Personal Physiological Anchor (PPA)
A physiology-locked eternal personal baseline (e.g., multi-modal biometric signals). It serves as the sovereign root-of-trust for the system.  Primary anchor tied to an individual.  

Generates revocable topological shards for secure multi-operator or distributed networks.  
Enables hardware-enforced human override and sovereignty.

Photonic Hardware Platform (TFLN Nodes)  Short-depth Dynamical String-Net Preparation (DSNP) circuits with EO modulators for dynamic reconfiguration.  

Ultra-low-loss waveguides, microrings, and co-integrated components.  

Dedicated ports for PPA calibration, Quantum Dot (QD) + Topological Insulator (TI) transduction, Photonic Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs), and Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs).  

Parametric GDSFactory scripts support military (rugged/radiation-hardened) and commercial (low-SWaP) variants.

Security and Resilience Features  Photonic PUF: MZI-mesh or scattering structures exploit manufacturing variations for unclonable device fingerprints.  

QRNG Integration: Provides true quantum randomness for challenges and key generation.  

Self-Reinforcing Topological Mechanism: Adversarial inputs (jamming, radiation, etc.) are encoded as Artin braids, inverted, and converted into surplus that strengthens invariants (Jones polynomials, Khovanov homology, etc.).  

Mirror Shard Mesh: Distributed non-local coordination with partial topological overlap to the primary PPA.  

Meta-RL Adaptation: Loss-penalized rewards, fidelity optimization, and dynamic reconfiguration that learns to compensate for real hardware effects.

Modeling and Simulations  Multi-agent decentralized swarm environments modeling drone/robot coordination with PPA shards.  

Photonic PUF challenge-response pairs (MZI mesh) with explicit modeling of voltage-induced EO phase shifts, thermal drift, and cumulative aging effects.  

Meta-RL for adaptive decoding, code discovery, and swarm resilience under stress.  

Results show strong coherence maintenance and adaptation despite degradation.

Intellectual Property
Provisional patent claims (neutral functional language) cover the physiology-locked topological fixed-point attractor, PUF integration, QD-TI transduction, loss mitigation, shard delegation, and multi-operator models. Multiple one-pager versions and proposal sections prepared for defense and dual-use pathways.

High-Value Applications Defense
Strong alignment with DoD Quantum and Battlefield Information Dominance (Q-BID) and related areas (trusted autonomy, contested logistics).

Key uses: Radiation-hardened GEO systems, jam-resistant C4ISR, sovereign swarm autonomy, resilient PNT/communications in contested EM environments. Excellent fit for Navy SBIR photonic resilience topics. Dual-Use Commercial Secure Physical AI and humanoid robotics (e.g., enhanced control, anti-tampering).  

Resilient drone swarms and autonomous vehicles.  
Hardware-rooted security for critical infrastructure and edge AI.  
Long-duration space commercialization.

text:
Scale-out CPO switches going from 5,000 units this year to 200,000 by 2030. And you are selling?

That’s a 144% CAGR. A 40x volume ramp that both Yole and Morgan Stanley are modeling off the same wall. That’s the whole thesis, and notice what it doesn’t require: it doesn’t require me to pick which laser vendor qualifies into which switch, or whether Broadcom’s socket beats Nvidia’s.

To be short this ramp up you have to argue the entire build doesn’t happen, and good luck making that case while the SemiAnalysis “delay” everyone panicked over still lands you at 200k units by 2030. 

A delay bends the curve doesn’t make it go away. 

The physics won’t let it go away. A 200,000 GPU cluster is burning ~17 megawatts just on transceivers. CPO cuts that ~73% per 800G. At scale-up density copper is already dead and pluggables are out of runway. Forced migrations are the best ramps you can be long because the demand doesn’t care.

The biggest winners:

Lasers and light sources: $LITE, $COHR, $SIVE
Optical engines and pump lasers: $AAOI, $COHR
Switch ASICs: $AVGO, $MRVL, $NVDA
Optical I/O chiplets: $ALAB, $CRDO
Packaging and assembly: $FN, $AMKR
InP substrate and materials: $AXTI

LITE is the cleanest CPO scarcity play, shipping 200G-per-lane EMLs at volume, book through 2028. $AAOI is the one that wins both ways: its core 800G and 1.6T pluggable business actually benefits if CPO slips, while its external laser fab keeps it in the game when CPO finally ramps.

Bullish photonics ⚡️

At scale-up density copper is already dead and pluggables are out of runway. Forced migrations are the best ramps you can be long because the demand doesn’t care. text:
2026/07/08
US market regime: 74/100
Strong, but slightly overheated. Reversal risk is rising.

Trend is flat. Bear alerts: 2/11.
The market is still strong, but momentum is slowing from recent highs. Major indices remain near the upper end of their 52-week range, while internal signals are getting mixed.

✅ Bullish factors:
Both cap-weighted and equal-weighted measures are moving higher
65% of stocks are above their 200DMA
Dollar-volume participation is 1.31x, now $882B, +9% vs 20 days ago and +40% over 3 months
Credit remains healthy
VIX term structure is calm
S&P 500 has a follow-through day signal

⚠️ Risk factors:
SPY has 7 distribution days
QQQ has 6 distribution days
Accumulation/distribution is 0.82, leaning toward distribution
Sentiment is 72, in greed territory
Leveraged ETF enthusiasm is elevated, with bull ratio at 77%
Offense/defense rotation is -1.1σ, slightly defensive
Nasdaq weakness is still visible

【Internals】
🔸Breadth: 65% above 200DMA = still healthy
🔸Participation: 1.31x / $882B = liquidity is expanding
🔸Accumulation/distribution: 0.82 = distribution bias. This is the main internal concern
🔸Leader strength: 68% = strong, but approaching overheated territory
🔸McClellan/RANA: +3.5 = neutral
🔸Offense/defense rotation: -1.1σ = slightly defensive
🔸Sentiment: 72 = greed

【Risk metrics】
Expected 1-month move, 1σ:
SPY ±4.7%, downside reference 713
QQQ ±8.1%, downside reference 652

Distribution days:
SPY 7 = correction warning
QQQ 6 = watch / caution

Sentiment:
Leveraged ETF heat is 93.
Parabolic stock ratio is 51.
SKEW is 146.

This is overheated, but not an automatic sell signal. Overheating is normal during strong uptrends.

【Capital rotation】
Capital is centered in:
semiconductors, manufacturing supply chains, and energy

Continued inflows:
payment networks, healthcare IT, fintech SaaS

Continued outflows:
lithium mining, AI optical/photonics, cable/satellite equipment

Key takeaway:
Participation and breadth remain strong. The liquidity inflow is real.
But accumulation/distribution is at 0.82, SPY and QQQ distribution days are piling up, and sentiment is getting hot.

So this is not a “buy everything because the market is strong” setup.
It is a selective market.

I’m focusing only on strong themes where pullback, volume, and relative strength line up.
No blind chasing here.

Not financial advice.

text:
Semiconductor & photonics names across the list took a broad hit today, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index shedding over 5% following a classic "sell-the-news" reaction to Samsung's record Q2 earnings — a 19-fold surge in operating profit that still fell short of elevated expectations, triggering aggressive profit-taking across AI-linked chip and optical stocks. Morgan Stanley's call to reduce semiconductor exposure and DeepSeek's reported development of an in-house AI inference chip added further headwinds to the sector.

AXT Inc.  was among the hardest hit, dropping -8.5% to $58.15 on elevated volume of over 8.3M shares. The pullback follows Monday's +19% two-day surge driven by a landmark $22.3M prepaid supply agreement with Coherent — that deal's fundamentals remain intact, and Northland Capital holds an Outperform rating with a $125 price target, but today's move reflects broader sector rotation rather than company-specific deterioration. Notably, AXTI still trades well off its 52-week high of $143.16, underscoring the volatility inherent in this high-beta AI wafer play.
Coherent Corp.  shed -6.4% to $314.13, giving back recent gains despite a strong fundamental backdrop — FQ3 revenue of $1.81B (+21% YoY), a $2B NVIDIA investment, and a Wall Street median price target of $372.50 all suggest the selloff is valuation-driven rather than thesis-breaking. Applied Optoelectronics  fell -7.3% to $114.41, compounded by mixed analyst sentiment (consensus Hold) and notable insider selling of over 500,000 shares in the past quarter. Sive  and 0ND2.L  also declined sharply at -11.7% and -12.7% respectively, consistent with the broader risk-off tone hitting smaller-cap names hardest.

The overarching theme is a multi-day whipsaw in AI hardware and semiconductor supply chain stocks — last week's selloff, Monday's partial rebound, and today's renewed pressure illustrate how sensitive these names remain to macro sentiment shifts around AI capex sustainability.

text:
Fascinating breakthrough from Osaka Metropolitan University on this phase-change magneto-optical metagrating device! Steering heat non-reciprocally at near-straight-on angles, separating absorption/emission, switching with GST, storing states power-free, and tuning via magnetic fields is huge for thermal photonics.
Here’s a speculative systems-level concept building directly on it for future high-performance 3D chip stacks (AI accelerators, HPC, etc.):
Hierarchical “Thermo-Magnetic Intelligent Packaging” Architecture
Chip-level (micro): Integrate small metagratings on the backside or edges of compute dies. Waste heat naturally drives resonant mid-IR (~13μm) emission. GST enables on/off latching of nonreciprocity; magnetic field provides continuous spectral/frequency tuning and steering. Modulated incoherent IR carries:
•  Operational control logic
•  Thermal status/hotspot data
•  Commands for dynamic management
•  Low-speed sideband/backup memory (non-urgent data, configs, logs, checkpoints)
This keeps the main silicon cores 100% dedicated to high-speed data processing—no control overhead.
Macro control level (package/board): A distributed network of Ni/Ti (Nitinol) elements handles global orchestration:
•  Automated feedback loops for thermal balancing across the entire stack.
•  Excess heat removal via actuated/curved paths through insulating seals to sinks.
•  Structural support and mechanical stability for the 3D assembly.
•  Field producers: Ni/Ti wires/structures generate or modulate the required transverse magnetic fields (~1T regionally) to steer the chip-level IR emission without placing magnets directly on delicate silicon.
•  Multi-tasking sensors (temperature via resistance/shape response) + electrical switching.
How it works in operation:
•  Chips generate heat → metagratings modulate and directionally steer IR (short-range only: mm to cm, as far as needed for local heat removal).
•  IR signal travels to macro detectors or adjacent layers, carrying control data.
•  Macro Ni/Ti network senses globally, actuates heat routing, adjusts magnetic fields for optimal steering, and balances load/heat across the stack in real time.
•  Closed-loop, low-power (non-volatile GST + shape-memory states), self-aware thermal management.
Benefits:
•  Turns waste heat into a useful control + sideband communication medium.
•  Massive parallelism (many extraction paths per chip/layer) boosts effective bandwidth for control traffic.
•  Clean separation: Compute optimized for speed; thermal/control offloaded to the “heat fabric.”
•  Improved resilience, predictive maintenance, and efficiency in dense 3D stacks.
•  Scalable with existing advanced packaging (interposers, hybrid bonding).
This isn’t a near-term product—just an extrapolation showing how your device’s nonreciprocal heat steering + non-volatile switching could enable truly intelligent, heat-utilizing systems. No extra laser heat bleed; everything rides on waste heat management.
What do you think—any other applications for programmable thermal radiation like this in sensors, energy harvesting, or photonics? Great work by the team! @OsakaMetUniv_en @physorg_com

text:
19:13 PM
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 (EDT)

I know, it's not my first time, my babysitter's should get onboard!

"Future enhancements under consideration include integration of closed-cycle helium cryogenic cooling with iridium-doped TFLN photonics. Cryogenic operation would further suppress thermal noise and enhance topological edge-state stability, while iridium doping provides complementary radiation hardening. 
👇🏼
Together, these would enable ultra-high-reliability operation in the most demanding missile defense and space environments."

This combination would push ForgeChip toward near-theoretical maximum resilience for photonic systems — extremely difficult to disrupt via physical, radiation, or thermal attacks. It’s a strong long-term vision, especially for high-end missile defense and space applications.

text: Not many people were active pre-Asia crisis. In July of ‘97 I was sent by my employer to live in Singapore for 2 months to help take down the Baht - I was 29. We spent the next several years buying cheap assets and selling them for incredible returns into the 00s. I don’t think people can comprehend the leverage and gambling mentality inherent in Asian bubbles.. I’m not even sure this is a bubble yet. But, the speculative money/structure unwind feels pretty orderly in Korea.  Obviously 40% sell-offs in single names - and there are many - doesn’t feel orderly, but this is not what one would call contagion. Iran aside, I have no clue if the QQQs will rally or the Kospi will rally from here/tonight, but I look at what’s been thrown at this market for the past few years and look where we are in the indexes & I don’t see fear. With all of this said, I don’t think fear is warranted at the moment.. and, like most, I have spent way too much time and effort and $$ over the past 6months trying to understand finite details of the Ai build out, bottlenecks, substrates, lasers, photonics, etc.  If anything, I think we have just ended that period of mental twister and for that, I am grateful.

text:
Everyone's thinking the same thing here: "what's happening to AI stocks?"

So, I'll explain what I think is happening.

And where I see us moving forward.

Let's deep dive:

I think most of these are going through a "distribution" formation.

Check the $SIVE chart below for what this means.

But basically, we have a sell off here, until we start accumulating (or showing signs of higher highs, and a it being a good spot people bid at).

Now we do have some fundamental reasons for this: 

Firstly, SemiAnalysis wrote a piece on CPO delays and company valuations. 

This caused the first sell off. 

$NVDA and other optical companies refuted these claims, though.

Secondly, we had a "sell the news" event on earnings and expections. 

$MU and Samsung's recent earning results were very strong, but it seems to not have been the massive beating investors were hoping for. 

So, this triggered sharp drops in memory and chip stocks ($MU, $SNDK, etc.), spilling over to the broader sector. Investors had priced in perfection.

I mean, check the past chart growth when this is really priced in for future growth % potential. 

Thirdly, we've had some "is this a bubble" fears.

AI infrastructure spending is enormous (hyperscalers like $MSFT , $GOOGL, $META, $AMZN on track for hundreds of billions), but questions linger on the short-term ROI. Is this sustainable?

Especially with the $META renting out $NBIS compute. 

People think it shows $META might not need as much compute in the future. So we saw a mass sell off on some names: $NBIS, $BRUN, $IREN, $HIVE, etc., but I don't think this will last.

I wrote about this earlier (just look at my earlier posts on $NBIS).

And enablers (such as $NVDA, $AMD, $AVGO, $MU, photonics plays like $SIVE, optical interconnects, power/cooling) ran up hugely on the buildout narrative. Profit-taking + rotation out of "expensive" tech is natural after crazy pumps (natural human psychology imo).

Fourth, we got some macro pressures holding us down (not limited to these companies, but they hurt the risk-on ones most): 

Interest rates/inflation: Fears of higher rates (Fed outlook, oil prices from geopolitics like Iran-related tensions) raise borrowing costs for the capital-intensive AI buildout. Debt financing for data centers and infra is surging too, so that doesn't help.

Geopolitical noise and strong jobs data have added volatility as well.

And lastly, as I'm writing this, news just broke out that the U.S. has started launching strikes against Iran again... so that's an added confluence we needed lol.

But, soon, I think we'll getting into the classic accumulation zone.

I think the $BTC -> AI pivot companies will hold best here, as crypto has bottomed imo.

But for the rest, I think we have a bit lower to go. Maybe just a tad.

In particular, $NBIS has a clear zone at 170 that I'm looking at for support. I'm hoping it holds there and accumulates for a bit before sending higher.

For $OPTX, I think this is in a consolidation phase, and $ASYS as well.

This really applies to all of these stocks (even $AAOI, $CRWV, and any other one you can think of).

We accumulate here before sending higher.

It's not going to be the "V" shape you all know and love.

But with due time, we will go up again imo.

text:
— Kyber NVL144, the flagship 2027 rack, delayed 12+ months to 2028. The PCB midplane can’t be manufactured at scale
— The backup architecture (NVL72x2) — cancelled
— The 4-die Rubin Ultra was already dead: packaging physically warps at that size. The 2-die survivor delivers roughly half the performance per package
— Optical scale-up (CPO) won’t arrive until Feynman

This is not one product slipping. It’s two physical walls — packaging AND interconnect — hitting in the same cycle. The “new rack every year” promise is what’s breaking.

WINNERS

— $GOOGL / $AVGO — the only proven alternative scale-up architecture on earth (optical switching, running in production for years). The 2027 window belongs to the TPU ecosystem
— $AMD — gets a window. A window is not a win. Execution at rack scale is unproven
— Pluggable optics: $COHR, $FN, $AAOI — CPO was the sword hanging over pluggables. It just got sheathed for another generation. The recent photonics selloff shot the wrong victim
— Copper: $APH, $CRDO — copper stays in the rack longer than anyone modeled
— Neoclouds: $NBIS, $CRWV — slower chip cadence means GPU fleets obsolete slower. The depreciation treadmill every bear cites just lost a year

LOSERS

— $NVDA — not revenue. They’ll sell every mature-gen rack they can make. What’s damaged is the moat premium: NVLink supremacy and the annual cadence ARE the multiple
— CPO pure plays: $POET, Sivers — the entire thesis is co-packaged optics adoption timing. That timing just moved right by a full generation. The biggest losers (Ayar Labs, Celestial AI) are private — you can’t even trade them
— $LITE — the trap. CPO laser story delayed, pluggable laser business extended. Net wash. Not the short people will think it is

THE $MU QUESTION

Do the math before you touch the sell button. Old Kyber rack: 144 packages x 16 HBM stacks = 2,304. New 2+2 config: 288 packages x 8 stacks = 2,304. Bit demand per rack: identical. What died is the “HBM per GPU doubles forever” narrative — a multiple story, not a bits story. And a flatter performance curve means buyers need MORE units for the same compute, in a market that’s supply-constrained until 2028.

Neutral on fundamentals. If it does trade down on the headline, then that gap will be called opportunity.

The nuance everyone will miss: NVIDIA can still hit rack-level compute targets with 2+2. Parity — bought with twice the silicon. The spec sheet survives. Cost per FLOP doesn’t.

The only question for 2027: can anyone exploit the gap before Feynman closes it? Exactly one company has a working alternative today.

Not financial advice.

text: https://t.co/eJZpNXVDA8

Bullish $SIVE: AMD is building CPO for the MI500 (2027) through GlobalFoundries, bypassing NVIDIA’s TSMC packaging bottleneck entirely. Since June, Sivers’ laser arrays are integrated into GF’s silicon photonics reference designs and SCALE CPO platform. If AMD’s optical scale-up bet materializes at GF, Sivers lasers are in the supply chain. NVIDIA stumbling widens exactly the window AMD and its photonics partners need.

Bullish $AAOI: every year CPO slips at NVIDIA is another year pluggable transceivers remain the workhorse of AI datacenters. Delays don’t destroy optical demand, they redirect capital back to pluggables and NPO. That’s AAOI’s core business. The market dumped it 17% on the delay headline without asking who actually benefits from it.

One company wins if CPO accelerates outside NVIDIA. The other wins because it’s delayed inside NVIDIA. Both win from the same news.

Delays don’t kill demand. They redistribute it.

text:
$AXTI $COHR Do you believe this is last InP prepayment deal that will be cut over the next 5 years? We merely getting started.
———————-
BOTTOM LINE: This is incrementally bullish for COHR fundamentals but more strategically important than financially material. It confirms that 6-inch InP substrate supply remains a gating item for COHR’s AI optical ramp, validates AXT/Tongmei as a meaningful 6-inch InP substrate supplier, and shows COHR is using customer-style capacity prepayments upstream to lock supply through the next 3 years. It should be more stock-moving for AXTI than COHR, but for COHR it is a positive de-risking datapoint after a weak tape day, not a thesis-changing event by itself.

The agreement is straightforward but important. AXT-Tongmei entered a 3-year Master Development and Supply Agreement with Coherent, effective June 25, 2026, covering “mass development and supply” of 6-inch indium phosphide wafer substrates. AXT will expand capacity at its Beijing facility from 2026 through 2028, Coherent will make a $22.2885M prepayment for committed capacity, wafer prices are pre-established, and the prepayment will be applied against future product purchases. Coherent also gets refund rights if unused amounts remain at expiry or termination, and can terminate and recover unused prepayment if AXT fails to meet the capacity commitment for more than 6 successive months. AXT gets protection if Coherent misses minimum order quantities, in which case unused prepayment becomes nonrefundable and AXT can terminate. AXT must also offer additional capacity beyond the committed quantity to Coherent on the same terms. The full agreement will not be available until AXT files its Q2 2026 10-Q.  

The key interpretation is that this is not about outsourcing COHR’s differentiated InP device manufacturing. AXT is supplying 6-inch InP wafer substrates, the upstream crystalline substrate input. Coherent still does the value-added photonic device work: EMLs, CW lasers, photodiodes, PICs and related integration. That distinction matters because COHR’s moat remains process/yield/device manufacturing and vertical integration; AXT is enabling more wafer starts, not replacing COHR’s 6-inch InP fab advantage.

For COHR, the positive read is supply-chain de-risking. Management has repeatedly framed InP capacity as the key constraint in the AI optical cycle, while also saying 6-inch InP is central to the revenue and gross margin ramp. In the May earnings call, COHR said its 6-inch platform was producing EMLs, CW lasers and photodiodes, with yields exceeding 3-inch lines, first 6-inch-derived transceiver shipments already contributing to revenue and margin, and internal InP capacity expected to double ahead of plan and more than double again by the end of calendar 2027.   This AXT agreement strengthens confidence that substrate availability will not become the hidden bottleneck that prevents those internal wafer starts from scaling.

This also lines up with the J.P. Morgan investor-meeting takeaway that COHR had substrate supply “locked in” across 5 suppliers spanning multiple geographies, several capable of producing 6-inch substrates. The AXT filing effectively names 1 of those upstream supply arrangements and shows there is real money, fixed pricing, and committed capacity behind the company’s prior substrate comments.  

The less bullish read is that the deal confirms the bottleneck is real and persistent. Coherent would not be making take-or-pay-like commitments and prepaying a supplier for 3-year capacity if 6-inch InP substrates were abundant. Reuters recently reported that China’s InP export controls have disrupted the AI photonics supply chain, that AXT and Sumitomo dominate global InP substrate production, that AXT is a major supplier to Coherent, and that 6-inch InP wafer prices have reportedly risen sharply since China tightened export licensing.   The agreement helps COHR, but it does not eliminate geopolitical risk because the capacity expansion is at AXT’s Beijing facility, precisely the jurisdiction where export licensing and materials controls have become a live issue.

The deal is financially immaterial to COHR in isolation. COHR’s market cap is roughly $65B and the $22.3M prepayment is de minimis against the equity value, capex program, and cash balance. It is best viewed as a capacity reservation / working-capital deposit, likely recorded as a prepaid asset and then consumed through substrate purchases, not as an immediate P&L event. COHR was trading near $333 after a roughly -9.6% move on July 2, so the stock reaction is more likely to be driven by AI hardware sentiment, valuation, and semiconductor tape than by a $22M supplier prepayment.

For AXTI, however, the signal is materially stronger. AXT reported Q1 2026 revenue of $26.9M, so a $22.3M prepayment is roughly 83% of a single quarter’s reported revenue, even though it is not immediately recognized as revenue and the total contract value could be meaningfully different from the prepayment. AXT also said in April that it had completed a $632.5M capital raise to support Tongmei’s InP capacity expansion and 6-inch InP R&D, so the COHR contract provides named strategic-customer validation for that capital deployment.   AXTI was down roughly -12.9% on July 2, and this after-close filing could support a rebound when regular trading resumes.

The most important subtlety is that the $22.3M should not be treated as the total revenue opportunity. The filing says the prepayment is applied against purchases and ties to committed capacity; it does not disclose wafer volumes, per-wafer prices, shipment cadence, gross margin, or whether additional capacity is expected to be purchased beyond the initial commitment. Using Reuters’ cited $5,000 average 6-inch InP wafer price as a purely illustrative benchmark, the prepayment would equate to roughly 4,500 wafers, but that calculation should not be used as a volume estimate because actual contract pricing is confidential and the prepayment may only represent a portion of the broader commitment.  

The fixed-price element is meaningful. If spot 6-inch InP pricing continues to rise, Coherent has partially hedged substrate cost and capacity access. That matters for the gross margin bridge because COHR’s >42% non-GAAP gross margin path relies on 6-inch cost/yield benefits, higher 1.6T mix, internal sourcing, and better absorption. J.P. Morgan’s May conference note highlighted 42%+ gross margin tailwinds from the shift from 800G to higher-margin 1.6T, the transition from 3-inch to 6-inch wafers, and movement from external to internal sourcing.   The AXT agreement supports that framework, but the benefit likely shows up gradually through wafer starts, device output, module shipments, and margin conversion rather than immediately in the next quarter.

This is also a useful read-through to AI optical demand. Coherent is not just receiving long-term customer commitments; it is passing those commitments upstream. That is how a real supply-constrained hardware cycle behaves. Customers commit to COHR, COHR commits to AXT, AXT expands Beijing 6-inch capacity, and the entire chain becomes increasingly governed by deposits, capacity reservations, fixed prices, minimum orders, and termination protections. This reinforces the view that the AI optical cycle is capacity constrained rather than purely speculative order inflation, though it does not disprove some double-ordering risk.

The agreement also has a China-risk wrinkle that investors should not ignore. Coherent has emphasized U.S. manufacturing scale, Sherman 6-inch InP leadership, CHIPS-related support, and the NVIDIA strategic partnership. This AXT agreement reveals that even a U.S.-anchored 6-inch InP device strategy still depends on upstream substrate inputs from China. That is not inherently negative, because AXT is a scaled and strategically important substrate supplier, but it means export permits, U.S.-China policy, force majeure language, and qualification of non-China supply remain critical diligence items.

Relative implications are clear. For COHR, this is a positive data point for execution confidence and supply visibility, but not enough on its own to revise estimates. For AXTI, it is a much bigger validation event because it confirms that a leading AI optical platform company is willing to prepay for Tongmei 6-inch substrate capacity. For LITE, the read-through is mixed: positive for the industry because InP scarcity is validated, but potentially negative at the margin if COHR is locking a larger share of scarce AXT 6-inch output. For the broader optical ecosystem, the deal supports the view that the scarcity value sits not only in finished transceivers but also deep upstream in compound semiconductor substrates, lasers, isolators, PM fiber, ELS modules, and other enabling components.

Trading implication: this should help stabilize the COHR narrative after the July 2 selloff, but it is not large enough to single-handedly re-rate the stock. The better near-term setup is probably AXTI up more sharply on customer validation and COHR modestly supported, with the caveat that U.S. markets are closed Friday, July 3 for Independence Day observed, so regular-session price discovery is delayed.   COHR remains a high-conviction structural AI optical beneficiary, but the stock still needs gross margin progression, 6-inch output conversion, and FCF inflection to justify the valuation. This announcement improves confidence in the supply-chain bridge to that outcome.

Key diligence questions for Monday/next week: whether this AXT capacity was already included in COHR’s “5 substrate suppliers” commentary or is incremental; whether export licenses are already secured; what share of FY2027 6-inch wafer starts this covers; whether the agreement includes first-priority capacity rights or merely committed capacity; whether the fixed wafer prices are below current spot; how quickly AXT can qualify volume 6-inch wafers to COHR specifications; and whether any of the prepayment is ultimately customer-funded through COHR’s own strategic LTAs.

For LITE, the read-through is mixed: positive for the industry because InP scarcity is validated, but potentially negative at the margin if COHR is locking a larger share of scarce AXT 6-inch output. For the broader optical ecosystem, the deal supports the view that the scarcity value sits not only in finished transceivers but also deep upstream in compound semiconductor substrates, lasers, isolators, PM fiber, ELS modules, and other enabling components. Trading implication: this should help stabilize the COHR narrative after the July 2 selloff, but it is not large enough to single-handedly re-rate the stock. The better near-term setup is probably AXTI up more sharply on customer validation and COHR modestly supported, with the caveat that U.S. markets are closed Friday, July 3 for Independence Day observed, so regular-session price discovery is delayed. COHR remains a high-conviction structural AI optical beneficiary, but the stock still needs gross margin progression, 6-inch output conversion, and FCF inflection to justify the valuation. This announcement improves confidence in the supply-chain bridge to that outcome. Key diligence questions for Monday/next week: whether this AXT capacity was already included in COHR’s “5 substrate suppliers” commentary or is incremental; whether export licenses are already secured; what share of FY2027 6-inch wafer starts this covers; whether the agreement includes first-priority capacity rights or merely committed capacity; whether the fixed wafer prices are below current spot; how quickly AXT can qualify volume 6-inch wafers to COHR specifications; and whether any of the prepayment is ultimately customer-funded through COHR’s own strategic LTAs."}}}, "quoted_status_result": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "core": {"user_results": {"result": {"__typename": "User", "affiliates_highlighted_label": {}, "avatar": {"image_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1996176688414367744/LXfA_lIx_normal.jpg"}, "core": {"created_at": "Wed Jul 02 10:44:15 +0000 2025", "name": "Serenity", "screen_name": "aleabitoreddit"}, "dm_permissions": {"can_dm": true}, "follow_request_sent": false, "has_graduated_access": true, "id": "VXNlcjoxOTQwMzYwODM3NTQ3NTY1MDU2", "is_blue_verified": true, "legacy": {"default_profile": true, "default_profile_image": false, "description": "Only on X, don’t trust fake accs AI/Semi Supply Chains NFA DYOR, no paid promos; may trade/hold names disc, views my own. text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — July 2, 2026

📊 SMH: $592.29 (-4.54%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• AXT-Tongmei signed a three-year 6-inch InP wafer substrate capacity agreement with Coherent, including a $22.3M prepayment tied to expanded Beijing capacity from 2026-2028. The optics supply chain is moving from spot-market scramble to locked capacity, which matters for Coherent, AXT, and the broader AI photonics bottleneck.
• Anthropic is exploring a custom AI chip with Samsung as a potential manufacturing partner, evaluating Samsung’s 2nm process and advanced packaging. If it moves forward, the read-through is bigger than one AI lab: frontier model companies are trying to control silicon roadmaps, and Samsung is still hunting for a marquee AI foundry win against TSMC.
• Nvidia is offering smaller GPU cloud providers financial backstops and revenue-share structures tied to unused GPU capacity. That supports near-term accelerator demand, but it also makes the AI infrastructure stack more circular: chip demand, cloud financing, and end-customer utilization are becoming increasingly intertwined.
• Meta said AI agent development over the last four months has not accelerated as expected, adding pressure to the capex ROI debate after reports that it may sell excess AI compute capacity. The takeaway is not that AI demand is dead; it is that hyperscaler capex now needs cleaner monetization proof, not just bigger buildout numbers.

text:
Hello darkness my old friend. 

Right when I think we’re done, you find a way to lure me back in!

I made peace with cutting $AXTI yesterday and it somehow finds a way to drag me back in. 

This deal is huge and the pre payment alone is nearly as much as their entire Q1 revenue. 

Not only that but it’s the catalyst it needs to shake off the recent fear around the AI infra trade and specifically photonics. 

This is my fifth time trying to trade AXTI in the past month. Is this time different?

text:
Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 2, 2026

Conexon Connect Georgia completed its ninth FTTH project with Satilla REMC, surpassing 240,000 homes/businesses, 5,200 fiber miles, and delivering multi-gig to ~60,000 locations across 9 counties. Vocus announced ~A$500M ducted Sydney-Melbourne long-haul (6,912 cores, ready 2029, AI-driven). Cloud Capital/Realty Income formed a $6B data center JV (Virginia). South Korea outlined $919B mega projects with 18.4GW data centers by 2035. Nixxy/Tachyon announced a 1GW North Dakota campus. Tata advanced India-Singapore subsea.

Ongoing AI hyperscaler demand drives fiber supply ramps (Corning, STL, CommScope, Clearfield, Fujikura tripling production) amid persistent shortages (preforms tight through 2026+). Regional OSP/ISP activity includes Ziply Northwest, Ripple Fiber WA, Ezee Fiber, Brightspeed WiFi 7, Burlington Telecom 5 Gbps, Heartland Fiber Project, FiberLight West Texas, Big Fiber financing, Atomic Fiber Los Alamos, and various BEAD/FTTH builds. Manufacturing focuses on high-count, multi-core, photonics; FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints add momentum.

 #FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

I believe this $AXTI Coherent wafer deal highlights the accelerating demand for indium phosphide photonics components that power AI data center optical interconnects. 

I think it positions the entire sector for strong growth as hyperscalers scale 800G and 1.6T deployments. $COHR benefits directly from secured supply while $LITE, $AAOI, and even broader plays like $AVGO stand to gain from the same ecosystem tailwinds in high-speed transceivers and lasers.

The market will re-rate them sooner than we anticipate 😎

text:
【量子ハードウェア】
Pasqalがカナダで光集積回路（PIC）実装拠点を新設。790万ドル（約11.9億円、1ドル=150円換算）規模で量子・センシング向け製造基盤を強化。
（2026年7月3日 日本時間、Quantum Computing Report）

何が起きたか：
Pasqalは、買収したカナダ子会社Aeponyxを通じて、ケベック州ブロモンのC2MI（MiQro Innovation Collaborative Center）内に、光集積回路（PIC）パッケージングの専門拠点を設立すると発表した。 

このプロジェクトは総額790万ドル（約11.9億円、1ドル=150円換算）で、うち400万ドル（約6.0億円）は連邦・州政府が支援し、その中にはNGen（Next Generation Manufacturing Canada）からの300万ドル（約4.5億円）が含まれる。 

拠点では、シリコンナイトライド（SiN）フォトニクスを活用したPICの実装・組立ラインを構築し、レーザー制御モジュールなど量子コンピュータや量子センシングに必要な光部品の量産体制を整える。初期は試作・少量生産を担い、将来的には年間50万個超のモジュール生産能力を目指す計画だ。HOP Technologies、Phantom Photonicsも参画し、医療センシングやLiDARなど量子以外の用途も視野に入れる。 

読み解き：
今回のポイントは「量子コンピュータを作る」ことではなく、「量子コンピュータを量産できる部品産業を作る」ことにある。

量子コンピュータではチップそのもの以上に、レーザー、光導波路、ファイバー接続などの光学実装が製造ボトルネックになりやすい。特に中性原子方式は多数のレーザーを高精度で制御する必要があり、PICの高品質な実装技術は性能だけでなく生産性にも直結する。 

興味深いのは、この拠点が量子専用工場ではなく、防衛向けLiDARや医療センシング企業も巻き込んだ共通インフラとして設計されている点だ。同じフォトニクス製造設備を複数産業で共有できれば、量子産業単独では難しい設備投資の採算性を高められる。近年は各国が「量子主権」を意識したサプライチェーン整備を進めているが、この発表も製造能力そのものを国内に確保する流れの一例と言える。Pasqalにとっても、Aeponyx買収後の垂直統合を具体的な製造基盤へ発展させる動きとして位置付けられる。 

評価：
・業界インパクト：★★★
・注目度：★★★
・関連銘柄：PASQAL（未上場）

結論：
量子業界ではプロセッサ性能だけでなく、「安定して作れる製造基盤」が競争力になり始めている。今後は各社が光部品やパッケージング、製造拠点への投資をどこまで拡大するかが、商用展開の速度を左右する重要な指標になりそうだ。

text:
【研究開発】
米NSFが量子フォトニクス実用化へ400万ドル追加支援。GPS不要の量子ナビゲーションを視野に、最終段階では最大5,500万ドル規模の量子センター構想へ発展する可能性。
（2026年7月3日 〔日本時間〕・The Quantum Insider）

何が起きたか：
米ミシガン大学が主導する量子フォトニクス研究プロジェクト「QuPID（Quantum Photonic Integration and Deployment）」が、米国立科学財団（NSF）のNational Quantum Virtual Laboratory（NQVL）プログラムのフェーズ2に採択され、2年間で400万ドル（約6億円、1ドル=150円換算）の助成を受けることになった。これはフェーズ1の100万ドル（約1.5億円）に続く資金で、将来的には最大5,500万ドル（約82.5億円）のNSF量子センターへ発展する可能性がある。研究では、量子状態の光を利用した集積フォトニクスチップを開発し、GPSが使えない水中・宇宙・月面などでも利用できる高精度ナビゲーションや超高精度センシングを目指す。プロジェクトにはオハイオ州立大学、ハーバード大学、スタンフォード大学、パデュー大学のほか、Honeywell、TOPTICA Photonics、General Motors、Toyota、Raytheon、NASA Glenn Research Center、Air Force Research Laboratoryなど産学官が参加している。 

読み解き：
このニュースの本質は、「量子コンピュータ」ではなく「量子フォトニクス」の商用化に研究資金の重心が移っている点にある。今回の目標は演算性能ではなく、量子光学を使った実環境向けセンサーやナビゲーションであり、量子技術をインフラへ組み込む流れを象徴している。

特にNSFがフェーズ制で資金を拡大し、最終的には数千万ドル規模の量子センターを構想していることは、単なる大学研究ではなく、量産・実装を前提としたエコシステム形成を狙っていることを示す。さらに、自動車メーカー、防衛企業、NASAまで参加していることから、出口市場が明確な研究テーマとして位置付けられている点も重要だ。

量子業界では計算機だけでなく、量子センシングや量子通信が商用化を先行する可能性が高いと見られており、今回の支援はその流れを裏付ける動きといえる。 

評価：
・業界インパクト：★★★
・注目度：★★★
・関連銘柄：HON, GM, TM

結論：
量子技術の競争軸は計算能力だけではなく、センシングやナビゲーションなど現実世界の用途へ広がりつつある。今後は、このような研究プロジェクトが実証実験や政府調達へ発展するかどうかが、商用化を見極める重要なポイントになる。

text:
The Market That Changed Engines Mid‑Flight Strategic & Tactical Read of the Day 

Today's tape told a story that wasn't subtle: the market didn't break, it pivoted. Momentum the great engine of the 2025–H1 2026 rally finally lost altitude. Semiconductors, memory, photonics, and the AI‑power complex all slipped into a decisive unwind. META admitted AI progress has been slower than expected; photonics rejected their 21‑DMA; semis logged a second straight 5%+ selloff; MTUM printed its sixth rare >3% down day while RSP rose. That's not noise that's regime change.

Yet beneath the surface, the bull market kept breathing. Defensive sectors surged: healthcare, staples, utilities, materials. Equal‑weight advanced. The Dow hit a record. Investors didn't de‑risk they reallocated. This is classic bull‑market rotation: money staying in equities, but choosing different leaders.

Macro reinforced the shift. Payrolls softened, real earnings remain pressured, and factory orders ex‑transport were solid a mix that tempers rate‑hike fears without signaling recession. Micro confirmed it: hyperscalers stabilizing, domestic cyclicals strengthening, and median earnings tracking double‑digit growth. The bottleneck trade is crowded; the broader market is not.

Strategically, leadership is migrating from AI‑momentum to diversified, cash‑flow‑rich, domestically leveraged sectors. Tactically, expect digestion in semis and selective strength in defensives until Q2 earnings clarify whether AI re‑accelerates or hands the baton to the rest of the market.

Bull markets don't die when leaders tire they evolve when new ones emerge.

And to all American readers: Happy Independence Day may your portfolios enjoy some fireworks too. 🇺🇸✨

text:
While AAOI’s primary business is building traditional pluggable optical transceivers, they also develop foundational high-power lasers and external laser sources for CPO architectures. So they’re bridging the gap today while also prepping for what comes next. CPO is a 2028/29 story at best. But the point is having a fundamental basis to justify a stock price today. Whether on a visible discounted cash flow basis or just material revenue/backlog growth TODAY. AAOI has explosive revenue growth actually materializing, on top of a surging **backlog**, whereas Sivers is baiting retail with a merely subjective **opportunity pipeline**, with zero photonics revenue today, let alone even a backlog to show for anything. Just non-exclusive partnerships on reference designs that they hope will be used by 2028. And pricing power will be fully at the discretion of WIN Semi, who they outsource designs to, further eroding potential margins should anything even manifest. I mean hey, this thing could surely pump again, but the risks are IMMENSE and it will only run on hype, which is intrinsically fizzling out (especially with all this dilution and some serious macro risks on the horizon/liquidity tightening). I suspect some funky business going on with their management, and I’m merely trying to make people aware not to blindly follow anonymous pump pages

text:
Paradis Labs ยังมั่นใจ $AAOI และ $AEHR แม้หุ้น AI Small Cap ปรับฐานแรง

Paradis Labs ระบุว่ายังคงถือหุ้น AAOI และ AEHR พร้อมแสดงความเชื่อมั่นต่อแนวโน้มระยะยาวของทั้งสองบริษัท แม้ว่าหุ้นกลุ่ม AI Infrastructure ขนาดเล็กจะถูกแรงขายกดดันในช่วงที่ผ่านมา

สำหรับ AAOI มองว่า

ตั้งเป้ารายได้จาก Optical Transceiver แตะประมาณ 471 ล้านดอลลาร์ต่อเดือนภายในกลางปี 2027
บริษัทกำลังขยายกำลังการผลิตรองรับผลิตภัณฑ์ 800G และ 1.6T
แม้เพิ่มกำลังผลิตแล้ว คาดว่าอุปสงค์จะยังสูงกว่ากำลังการผลิตจนถึงประมาณไตรมาส 3 ปี 2027

ส่วน AEHR

เพิ่งได้รับคำสั่งซื้อเพิ่มเติมสำหรับระบบ Wafer Level Burn-In (WLBI) จากลูกค้า Silicon Photonics
มีโอกาสได้รับคำสั่งซื้อเพิ่มภายในปีนี้ หลังผ่านการรับรองเป็นซัพพลายเออร์แล้ว
ก่อนหน้านี้ยังได้รับคำสั่งซื้อระบบ Package Level Burn-In (PLBI) จากลูกค้า Hyperscaler รายใหญ่

Paradis Labs ระบุว่า หุ้นทั้งสองตัวมีความผันผวนสูง (High Beta) จึงถูกแรงขายในรอบ AI Selloff มากกว่าหุ้นขนาดใหญ่ แต่ยังเชื่อมั่นในแนวโน้มระยะยาวและยังคงถือการลงทุนต่อ

I have high conviction in $AAOI and $AEHR.

And still hold my positions.

With $AAOI:
> Target ~$471M monthly transceiver revenue mid-2027.
> Capacity ramp is happening now for 800G/1.6T.
> Even with capacity expansion, demand is expected to exceed production capacity until ~Q3

text:
Be warned!! This is a little long but VERY informative on who Kopin should be prospecting and timeline of execution.

I KNOW we are early on $KOPN with its new MicroLED Neural I/o chip design. And its DEMO to be presented end of 2026. So trying to take advantage while their is time, #KOPIN should be looking into hyperscalers first. 

Hyperscalers & Cloud AI Builders (Top Priority)

NVIDIA (GPU clusters, NVLink complements), Google (TPUs), Amazon (Trainium/Inferentia), Microsoft (Azure Maia/custom silicon), Meta, Oracle, xAI, and others building massive AI training/inference clusters.

WHY? 
They face acute power and bandwidth walls in dense GPU racks. Power/cooling is a top TCO driver; Neural I/o promises major efficiency gains for intra-rack or short inter-rack GPU-to-GPU and GPU-to-memory traffic.
AI Chip/Accelerator Designers
NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom (custom ASICs), and startups (Cerebras, Groq, SambaNova, etc.). Also advanced packaging players (TSMC, Intel, Samsung) for chiplet or co-packaged optics (CPO) integration.

WHY? 
Direct integration into next-gen accelerators or 2.5D/3D packages. Supports high-parallelism needs without heavy SerDes bottlenecks.

Server/System OEMs & Integrators Supermicro, Dell, HPE, Lenovo.

WHY? 
They build the racks/servers sold to hyperscalers. Better interconnects enable higher-density, lower-power systems that differentiate their offerings. Networking & Photonics Ecosystem Broadcom (AVGO—networking ASICs/optics leader), Arista, Cisco, plus photonics firms (Lumentum/LITE, Coherent). Potential partners or complementors rather than pure competitors for specific short-reach use cases.

Defense, Government & Sovereign AI Infrastructure

DoD/intelligence community data centers, contractors, and sovereign AI projects (Europe, Middle East, Asia) seeking secure/domestic supply chains.

WHY?
Kopin’s US-based full MicroLED production capability + long defense heritage (displays, soldier systems) provides a strong moat for secure AI hardware. Dual-use potential with existing defense MicroLED work.

Advantages of $KOPN vs. Current Data Center Interconnects

Neural I/o sits in a “sweet spot” between copper’s limitations and traditional laser optics’ drawbacks for short-to-medium reach, high-bandwidth AI intra-cluster links. It draws from “wide-and-slow” parallel channel concepts (hundreds/thousands of lower-speed MicroLED channels instead of a few ultra-high-speed lanes), similar to Microsoft Research’s MOSAIC project (which demonstrated ~56-68% lower power, up to 50m reach, and much higher reliability using MicroLEDs + imaging fiber).Neural I/o wins on bandwidth scalability (parallelism enables 1.6 Tbps demo target soon, 3.6 Tbps roadmap), dramatically lower power per bit, less heat (easier cooling, higher rack density), and better reach for board/rack scales. It “breaks the copper wall” while keeping costs and power closer to copper-like economics for these reaches.Vs. Traditional Optical (InP/VCSEL lasers, silicon photonics transceivers, pluggable optics or CPO):
Lasers excel at longer reach but are power-hungry, expensive, thermally sensitive, complex (heavy DSP, CDR, FEC, equalization, SerDes), and overkill (plus higher failure rates) for many short intra-rack or chip-to-chip links in AI clusters. They add cost, power, and complexity where not fully needed.

Neural I/o wins by using MicroLEDs as direct emitters (cheaper, lower power, more temperature-robust, simpler drive electronics possible with parallel low-speed channels). It eliminates or reduces need for expensive laser modules and complex digital backends for targeted use cases. Potential for smaller form factor, higher reliability/redundancy (pixel-level), and better power efficiency (comparable to MOSAIC’s ~3-5W vs. 10-12W per link range in research). Bidirectional capability from NeuralDisplay architecture is a plus. It avoids “expensive laser-based systems entirely” for these applications while delivering needed bandwidth.

LOOONG $KOPN

text:
Daily Buzzberg Alpha

the tape isn't sending one macro signal. it's repricing a regime: geopolitical supply risk returning while ai capex becomes an earnings tailwind AND an inflation/rates risk at the same time.

that combination kills generic beta and creates dispersion. the market has stopped paying for ai exposure or risk-on exposure — it's paying for contracted demand, validated supply-chain position, and balance-sheet capacity. it's punishing crowded trades where the thesis is intact but the holder base is impaired.

the whole note reduces to one filter: contracts > stories.

— where the asymmetry sits —

$WYFI — cleanest setup in the neocloud wreckage. ~$1b+ contracted backlog vs ~$1.3b market cap, and nscale's $900m credit facility validates the counterparty. sold with the basket, priced like the contracts don't exist.

$AVGO — the one ai print this week backed by a signed deal: apple's $30b+ agreement + $1.5b colorado buildout. validates the custom asic layer beyond gpu scarcity. revenue visibility, not vibes — and the cleaner read-through than $AAPL, which carries higher domestic cost and eu overhang.

$ONDS — thesis tied to numbers, not narrative: path to ~$1b revenue by 2027, net-income inflection, sum-of-parts floor, q2 print as the catalyst.

photonics — the under-the-surface one. inp substrates easing from "very tight" to "tight" isn't bearish — it lets downstream optical names convert interconnect demand into shipments. distinguish upstream bottleneck vs downstream qualification moats. $LITE $AAOI $COHR

$RKLB — flow unusually specific: 200dma entry, ms overweight $105 pt, vertical-integration bull case. blue origin's $130b private raise now anchors every public space comp.

— crowded, entry decayed —

$PENG — textbook signal decay. real discovery a month ago (earnings beat, cxl exposure, nvidia ai factory partner). now the flow is victory laps and partial trims. thesis quality high, entry quality gone.

broad ai beta — warsh's divided fomc tied ai capex to upside inflation risk, some members already floating hikes. that's the mechanism: the buildout that feeds suppliers also keeps real rates sticky, so long-duration ai without contract visibility underperforms. $TLT stays exposed.

— battleground —

memory — highest-dispersion area on the tape. korean flow reads the samsung/hynix selloff as forced deleveraging (levered-etf + retail-credit mechanics, adr re-rating catalyst) with hbm4/rubin demand structural through 2028. us flow is chaos — "memory is dead" next to aggressive $MU $SNDK dip buys. right framing: thesis intact, ownership base damaged. tradable, not clean.

oil — the move isn't the crude print, it's the transmission. brent ~$80 is the tactical level where short-covering extends, but the real signal is hormuz uncertainty leaking into diesel, propane, shipping and reserves — inflation pass-through that survives even if front-month crude stalls. own the complex where the physical market stays tight. $USO $CRAK

also on the desk: $NBIS washed-out sub-$170 but the bar is now contracted utilization, $META's $10b canada dc arguing against a capex stop, $OUST physical-ai perception via nvidia jetson, $HOOD chain optionality (early, distribution call not revenue).

bottom line: the tape is moving from "ai trade" to "ai underwriting." it pays receipts and punishes stories — $AVGO and $WYFI have them, $PENG had them last month, memory has them for 2028 but not for this week.

text: Nvidia $NVDA CEO Jensen Huang has announced a litany of partnerships over the past year focused on data centers, photonics technology, and more. I compiled a list of those partnerships and why each matters. https://t.co/D2NBUD74Uj

The Big Five are spending on AI faster than they're earning: @Microsoft, @Google, @amazon, @Meta. If CapEx exceeds cash flow, it can only persist as long as investors keep funding it. @PeterDiamandis asked me: What happens if sentiment shifts?

Here's my take: sentiment won’t shift. If anything, the investment will become more justified over time.

The world still sees compute as a rapidly depreciating asset but that’s simply not what it is anymore. Short of a massive leap in photonics or quantum computing, GPUs will continue to be highly sought after and have a predictable service life. This is already turning GPUs hours into a commodity.

Think about it like buying a house: when you buy a house, you finance it, and the house is collateral. In a world where compute becomes acceptable collateral, the hyperscalers could easily raise 10x to 100x on their cash flow in equity and debt, if they choose to.

In reality, there’s a lot of investable dollars in the world that want to flow into AI but can’t. AI is still the largest wealth creation moment in the history of humankind.

If CapEx exceeds cash flow, it can only persist as long as investors keep funding it. @PeterDiamandis asked me: What happens if sentiment shifts? Here's my take: sentiment won’t shift. If anything, the investment will become more justified over time. The world still sees compute as a rapidly depreciating asset but that’s simply not what it is anymore. Short of a massive leap in photonics or quantum computing, GPUs will continue to be highly sought after and have a predictable service life. This is already turning GPUs hours into a commodity. Think about it like buying a house: when you buy a house, you finance it, and the house is collateral. In a world where compute becomes acceptable collateral, the hyperscalers could easily raise 10x to 100x on their cash flow in equity and debt, if they choose to. In reality, there’s a lot of investable dollars in the world that want to flow into AI but can’t. text:
Spot on. $SIVE is textbook.

Retail got positioned early on the AI photonics thesis, institutions finally show up (JP Morgan 5%+), then boom, dilution shakeout + noise drops it 65% from the highs while the pipeline and partnerships keep firing.

Swedish sellers out, US buyers (and big money) stepping in on the cheap. Classic transfer.
The narrative isn’t dead, it’s just getting loaded quietly. AI data center optics demand isn’t pausing.
Backing up the truck on these dips.

text:
Institutional Lenders Just Swapped $12M in Debt for $SIVE Equity at the Bottom. If You’re Selling $SIVE Here, You’re Trading Against the Smart Money.

Until a month ago, everyone was bullish on $SIVE at the top. Now everyone is max bearish because it’s down 55% from ATH. Classic retail psychology. People see red, panic, and completely miss the structural reality of what’s actually happening beneath the surface for $SIVE.

If you are short $SIVE here, you're basically betting the entire AI data center physical layout is going to grind to a halt. But look at how the puzzle pieces are locking together right now for $SIVE:

The Silicon Valley Pure-Play Carve-Out: The crowd is totally blind to this value play. $SIVE is merging its hyper-growth photonics business with Nordic Acquisition to spin it off into a standalone, U.S. Nasdaq-listed entity. By liberating this unit into the heart of the tech ecosystem, $SIVE is putting its indispensable Indium Phosphide (InP) laser platform right in front of deep-pocketed American AI capital while keeping controlling ownership.

The Institutional Vote of Confidence: Skeptics keep crying about paper dilution, but they completely ignored what just crossed the wire. A top-tier institutional lender (Bootstrap Europe) chose to convert their entire $12M loan straight into ordinary $SIVE equity at these exact lower levels. Smart money didn't want their cash back; they took 22 million shares of equity instead because they know the optical supplier thesis is fully intact.

The Weaponized Balance Sheet: That recent SEK 700 million directed share issue wasn’t generic fundraising, it was a calculated move. Equipment lead times for high-purity cleanrooms are hovering at 9 to 12 months. $SIVE proactively secured the cash today to expand its InP manufacturing capacity. If you aren’t spending that CAPEX right now, you completely miss the 2027 demand curve.

The Reality Check: Near-term execution risks, corporate restructuring friction, and high beta mean localized volatility for $SIVE will stay extreme. Microcaps transitioning into multi-entity setups always face messy trailing quarters before peak commercial production volume ramps late next year.

But if you look past the standard retail panic, $SIVE just systematically wiped out its external debt, capitalized its capacity expansions, and established a direct pathway to Silicon Valley valuation multiples. This isn't a structural breakdown for $SIVE, it is a massive accumulation window.

Holding my core $SIVE positions without a single trim.
