text:
Dear @TeamKeerthanaTN, @Keerthana4VNR, @Guidance_TN 
For Keerthana Office, CMO Tamil Nadu:

In the next 120 days, Tamil Nadu should run a “Central Fund Capture Mission” to bring the next wave of high-value investments in Deep Tech, Semiconductors, EVs, Drones, Defence, Electronics and Advanced Manufacturing. Please out compete Andhra Pradesh, they are going all out on a 24 by 7 campaign to bring all the industries to their state.

Should of Office of Keerthana also travel to Delhi for 3-4 days and talk to PMO office, and other Central Ministers?

Please check the speech of Lokesh Nara, they have a strong framework. Please hire BCG, Bain Consultants including experts from IIT-M, IIM-Tiruchirapalli, IIM-A if need be.

Top 15 Central Government funds/schemes TN can aggressively leverage:

🏗️ SASCI – Special Assistance to States for Capital Investment: Use 50-year interest-free Centre loans for industrial roads, power, water, logistics, plug-and-play parks and port-linked manufacturing zones.

🏭 BHAVYA Industrial Parks Scheme: Compete for new integrated industrial parks and position TN for sector-specific parks in EVs, electronics, defence, aerospace, drones and precision manufacturing.

🔬 India Semiconductor Mission / Semicon India: Build TN’s semiconductor pitch around fabs, ATMP/OSAT, compound semiconductors, display tech, chip design and supplier ecosystems.

⚙️ Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme: Target component makers for PCBs, camera modules, sensors, connectors, power electronics and mobile/electronics supply chains around Chennai–Sriperumbudur–Hosur–Coimbatore.

💻 Electronics / IT Hardware Manufacturing Incentives: Attract laptop, server, tablet, telecom device and data-centre hardware manufacturers using TN’s electronics talent and port advantage.

🚗 PLI Auto & Auto Components: Push TN as India’s EV + advanced automotive technology capital for e-2W, e-3W, e-4W, e-buses, ADAS, motors, controllers, powertrain and auto electronics.

🔋 PLI ACC Battery Storage: Bring cell manufacturing, battery packs, BMS, recycling, lithium/Na-ion innovation and grid-scale battery projects to Hosur, Chennai, Coimbatore and southern TN.

⚡ PM E-DRIVE Scheme: Use central support for EV adoption and public charging to make Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy and industrial corridors EV-ready investment zones.

🚁 Drone PLI Scheme: Create a TN Drone Manufacturing & Testing Cluster for components, UAV assembly, agri-drones, defence drones, coastal surveillance drones and Drone-as-a-Service startups.

🛡️ iDEX + ADITI Defence Innovation: Connect TN Defence Industrial Corridor firms with startups, MSMEs, IIT-Madras, universities and DRDO-linked projects for cutting-edge defence technologies.

🎯 DRDO Technology Development Fund: Help TN companies pursue grants for indigenous defence systems, sensors, avionics, naval tech, aerospace components and dual-use deep-tech products.

🧠 IndiaAI Mission: Build TN AI compute, AI startup, data, skilling and applied AI programs for healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, governance, agriculture and Tamil-language AI.

🚀 ANRF / RDI ₹1 lakh crore Deep-Tech Fund: Position IIT-M, Anna University, research parks, startups and industries to capture long-tenor R&D capital for high-risk, high-impact innovation.

🌌 National Quantum Mission + Space VC Funds: Create TN quantum, photonics, sensing, satellite, propulsion and space-tech clusters linking Chennai, Coimbatore, Tirunelveli and Mahendragiri ecosystem.

🧵 PM MITRA + Startup India FoF / Seed Fund: Accelerate Virudhunagar textile park, technical textiles, manufacturing startups, deep-tech startups and MSME scale-up through Centre-backed startup and cluster capital.

Action for TN:

✅ Create a Central Scheme War Room
✅ Map each scheme to a TN district/cluster
✅ Prepare investor-ready project notes
✅ Add State incentives + land + power + approvals
✅ Pitch global investors with Centre + State package

Tamil Nadu can become India’s #1 destination for deep-tech manufacturing if every central scheme is converted into a targeted investment pipeline.

Please do also reach out to the Central Government and @svembu  to connect across India and abroad.

@TVKVijayHQ @CMOTamilnadu @JhonArokiasamy @arunraajkg @LoyolaMani @VijayadharaniM @Vijayabaskarofl  @iitmadras @IIM_Trichy @svembu @Zoho @annamalai_k @WTLFoundation

Should of Office of Keerthana also travel to Delhi for 3-4 days and talk to PMO office, and other Central Ministers? Please check the speech of Lokesh Nara, they have a strong framework. Top 15 Central Government funds/schemes TN can aggressively leverage: 🏗️ SASCI – Special Assistance to States for Capital Investment: Use 50-year interest-free Centre loans for industrial roads, power, water, logistics, plug-and-play parks and port-linked manufacturing zones. 🏭 BHAVYA Industrial Parks Scheme: Compete for new integrated industrial parks and position TN for sector-specific parks in EVs, electronics, defence, aerospace, drones and precision manufacturing. 🔬 India Semiconductor Mission / Semicon India: Build TN’s semiconductor pitch around fabs, ATMP/OSAT, compound semiconductors, display tech, chip design and supplier ecosystems. ⚙️ Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme: Target component makers for PCBs, camera modules, sensors, connectors, power electronics and mobile/electronics supply chains around Chennai–Sriperumbudur–Hosur–Coimbatore. 💻 Electronics / IT Hardware Manufacturing Incentives: Attract laptop, server, tablet, telecom device and data-centre hardware manufacturers using TN’s electronics talent and port advantage. 🚗 PLI Auto & Auto Components: Push TN as India’s EV + advanced automotive technology capital for e-2W, e-3W, e-4W, e-buses, ADAS, motors, controllers, powertrain and auto electronics. 🔋 PLI ACC Battery Storage: Bring cell manufacturing, battery packs, BMS, recycling, lithium/Na-ion innovation and grid-scale battery projects to Hosur, Chennai, Coimbatore and southern TN. ⚡ PM E-DRIVE Scheme: Use central support for EV adoption and public charging to make Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy and industrial corridors EV-ready investment zones. 🚁 Drone PLI Scheme: Create a TN Drone Manufacturing & Testing Cluster for components, UAV assembly, agri-drones, defence drones, coastal surveillance drones and Drone-as-a-Service startups. 🛡️ iDEX + ADITI Defence Innovation: Connect TN Defence Industrial Corridor firms with startups, MSMEs, IIT-Madras, universities and DRDO-linked projects for cutting-edge defence technologies. 🎯 DRDO Technology Development Fund: Help TN companies pursue grants for indigenous defence systems, sensors, avionics, naval tech, aerospace components and dual-use deep-tech products. 🧠 IndiaAI Mission: Build TN AI compute, AI startup, data, skilling and applied AI programs for healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, governance, agriculture and Tamil-language AI. 🚀 ANRF / text:
Lol… Non exclusive only matters when there is a second supplier.

Who exactly are they qualifying?

$GFS can qualify a second supplier whenever they want. But there is no known alternative in their qualification pipeline right now.

InP DFB lasers at this spec and volume is one of the hardest things to manufacture in photonics.

Non exclusive only matters if a credible replacement exists.

text:
$SIVE and $LITE are both down badly in the last month. 

Don't understand the heavy criticism around only Sivers if the overall photonics sector is going down and with bearish sentiment.

Wouldn't go against photonics. This a long-term story that will show really strong potential in 2027 and beyond. TAM expansion is huge. Constraints related to supply are there to do the talking.

It is always wise to size in according to different risk profiles. The market could stay down for a few more weeks. Although, I do believe around end of July, things will look really good due to earnings coming in from multiple corners. This seems like another short-term correction to remove weak hands.

Believe long-term!

P/S: ~4.7x FY2024/25 sales
Q4 FY26 sales: €202M, down 36%
FY26 free cash flow: €63M

Soitec is not a hype startup. It is a real semiconductor materials company producing advanced substrates used in mobile, automotive, industrial, Edge AI and Cloud AI applications.

The bull case is pretty clear: AI data centers need more advanced materials, photonics, energy efficiency and substrate innovation. Soitec has exposure to Photonics-SOI, FD-SOI, SmartSiC and other advanced wafer technologies.

The bear case is also clear: mobile is still weak, automotive is still soft, SmartSiC has been pressured by Chinese competition, and the stock already re-rated hard from the lows.

So this is not “cheap because it dropped.”

But it is definitely a name I want to watch closely.

A real European semiconductor materials company, 50% below its 52-week high, with AI / photonics exposure and positive free cash flow again.

Interesting setup.

$SOI.PA $SLOIY

NFA.

text:
THE AI REVOLUTION MARCHES ON
Alex's Track Record is Spot On
Here's how he suggests you might win:

$COHR (Coherent Corp)
Significant recent decline shown. Coherent is a key player in photonics, lasers, and optics — critical for high-speed data transmission and advanced semiconductor manufacturing in AI data centers. NVIDIA $NVDA has invested in the company for data center optics.

$IREN (IREN Ltd / Iris Energy)
Sharp drop highlighted. This is an AI/HPC-focused data center and sustainable computing play (Bitcoin mining + expanding into AI cloud infrastructure). Alex has been bullish on it before as a "NeoCloud" beneficiary of AI compute demand.

Other AI/tech stocks shown in Alex's video:

$AMD — AI GPU/chip competitor and data center player.
$INTC (Int:l) — Semiconductor leader pushing into AI, foundry services, and data center chips.

text:
🚀  $SIVEF  🚀 

The ABC retracement in Wave C got extended but I believe we are close to a reversal.
I’m very bullish on this Ai core play who’s a provider of laser and packaged optics.

Daily chart 👇
Stopped on my line !
RSI low but not oversold, because the underlying trend strength is so strong. 
We already reach the max fib retracement 61.8%.
4H chart 👇
Bottom signal didn’t confirm and no bullish divergence on the RSI yet.
I will wait for a reversal sign to add.

NEWS
- Dilution announced on July 1st
- The company is actively preparing for a U.S. Nasdaq listing which could be a big catalyst for the next wave.

After looking at the chart closer I changed my wave count :
I now think that this correction is large cycle II with large wave III coming soon with targets $14-$16.50
Extended $19.50-$21.50

text:
かつて協働研究を行なっていた文化人類学者の宮永國子氏は、ワークショップに一時参加していた古河電工のベテランエンジニアの方を、「彼は天才です。」と言っていたのを思い出した。 🌹Grok『古河電工（古河電気工業）の基幹的イノベーションテクノロジーは、創業以来一貫して「メタル（Metals）」「ポリマー（Polymers）」「フォトニクス（Photonics）」「高周波（High Frequency）」の4つのコア技術を軸としています。これらは電線製造技術から自然に派生・進化したもので、現在も同社の全事業の基盤となっています。
4つのコア技術の概要
メタル: 銅精錬・金属加工技術から発展。導電材、超電導線材、カーボンナノチューブ、高機能銅箔・条など。
ポリマー: 絶縁材・機能樹脂の開発技術。自動車部品、放熱材、半導体製造用テープ、機能性フィルムなど。
フォトニクス: 光ファイバー・光デバイス・レーザー技術。情報通信の基幹を担い、近年は加工・医療分野にも拡大。
高周波: RF・高周波電子技術。無線通信部品、自動車電子部品、高速基板材料など。
これらを単独で深化させるだけでなく、融合させて社会課題解決型の新事業を創出している点が現在の特徴です。
過去の基幹的イノベーション（歴史的マイルストーン）
古河電工のイノベーションは、電線・電力インフラから情報通信・先進材料へという大きなシフトが特徴です。
1884年創業〜戦前: 足尾銅山由来の銅精錬から電線製造へ。日本初の海底ケーブル（1915年）や無装荷海底ケーブル（1937年、世界初）など、メタル＋ポリマーの組み合わせで社会インフラを支えました。
1974年: 世界初の光ファイバーケーブルフィールド試験に成功。これがフォトニクス事業の本格的な起点となりました。
1983年: 世界初の実用光ファイバ複合OFケーブルの商業化。
2001年: 米Lucent Technologiesの光ファイバ部門（OFS）買収。グローバル規模でフォトニクス技術・特許を強化。
2000年代〜2010年代: 超電導線材の開発が加速。CERN（欧州合同原子核研究機構）から特別賞受賞（2003年、LHC用）。2016年には希土類系超電導線材による永久電流技術を開発（MRIなど医療応用）。
2015年: 世界トップクラスの導電率を持つカーボンナノチューブの開発成功（メタル分野の先進材料イノベーション）。
この時期は「銅の会社から光の会社へ」の転換期であり、4コア技術の枠組みが明確に確立されました。
現在の基幹的イノベーションと応用
現在は4コア技術の深化＋融合＋デジタル化がキーワードです。古河電工グループVision 2030では、「情報・エネルギー・モビリティを組み合わせた次世代インフラソリューション」を目指しています。
1. フォトニクス分野の最先端
超多心・細径光ファイバケーブル: 2018年に世界最高水準のコア密度を実現した6912心細径超多心ケーブルを開発。以降も進化を続け、2026年3月には13824心（従来比約2倍容量）の量産を開始（AIデータセンター向け）。データ爆発（生成AI、5G/6G）に対応する基幹技術です。
光デバイス: 狭線幅・高出力マイクロITLA（2017年、100Gbps超光コヒーレント通信用）。
レーザー加工: Blue-IRハイブリッドレーザー「BRACE™」（2020年発売、日亜化学工業との協業）。青色レーザーと近赤外ファイバレーザーを組み合わせ、銅のスパッタレス高品質溶接を実現。EVバッテリーや電子部品製造に不可欠で、近年は5kW級青色レーザーモジュールまで進化。
2. メタル＋先進材料分野
超電導技術: 高温超電導集合導体の研究開発（NEDOプロジェクト）。浮体式洋上風力発電向け超高圧ダイナミック海底ケーブル（2019年受注）。核融合エネルギーや産業用大型電磁石への応用も進行中（2026年現在、英国Tokamak Energyとの協業も発表）。
軽量・高導電材料: カーボンナノチューブのワイヤ応用など。
3. ポリマー・高周波の応用
自動車電装システム（ワイヤハーネス、ステアリングロールコネクタ、先進安全センサー）。
放熱・冷却部品、半導体製造用テープ、高周波基板用銅箔など。EV・自動運転・5Gインフラを支えています。
4. 横断的イノベーション（特に重要）
DX（デジタルトランスフォーメーション）: 「デジタルトランスフォーメーション＆イノベーションセンター（DXIC）」を中核に、AI/IoTを製造現場に本格適用。SAP S/4HANA基幹システム刷新やデータ駆動型ものづくりを推進。
オープンイノベーション: 「Fun Lab®」をプラットフォームに、NEDOプロジェクト（金属積層造形へのフォトニクス活用など）やスタートアップ連携を積極化。
新分野展開: ライフサイエンス（光プローブやレーザー医療機器、蛍光シリカナノ粒子を用いた高感度検査キット）、インフラ維持管理、エアロスペースなどへ4コア技術を応用。』

2018年に世界最高水準のコア密度を実現した6912心細径超多心ケーブルを開発。以降も進化を続け、2026年3月には13824心（従来比約2倍容量）の量産を開始（AIデータセンター向け）。データ爆発（生成AI、5G/6G）に対応する基幹技術です。 光デバイス: 狭線幅・高出力マイクロITLA（2017年、100Gbps超光コヒーレント通信用）。 レーザー加工: Blue-IRハイブリッドレーザー「BRACE™」（2020年発売、日亜化学工業との協業）。青色レーザーと近赤外ファイバレーザーを組み合わせ、銅のスパッタレス高品質溶接を実現。EVバッテリーや電子部品製造に不可欠で、近年は5kW級青色レーザーモジュールまで進化。 2. メタル＋先進材料分野 超電導技術: 高温超電導集合導体の研究開発（NEDOプロジェクト）。浮体式洋上風力発電向け超高圧ダイナミック海底ケーブル（2019年受注）。核融合エネルギーや産業用大型電磁石への応用も進行中（2026年現在、英国Tokamak Energyとの協業も発表）。 軽量・高導電材料: カーボンナノチューブのワイヤ応用など。 3. ポリマー・高周波の応用 自動車電装システム（ワイヤハーネス、ステアリングロールコネクタ、先進安全センサー）。 放熱・冷却部品、半導体製造用テープ、高周波基板用銅箔など。EV・自動運転・5Gインフラを支えています。 4. 横断的イノベーション（特に重要） DX（デジタルトランスフォーメーション）: 「デジタルトランスフォーメーション＆イノベーションセンター（DXIC）」を中核に、AI/IoTを製造現場に本格適用。SAP S/4HANA基幹システム刷新やデータ駆動型ものづくりを推進。 オープンイノベーション: 「Fun Lab®」をプラットフォームに、NEDOプロジェクト（金属積層造形へのフォトニクス活用など）やスタートアップ連携を積極化。 新分野展開: ライフサイエンス（光プローブやレーザー医療機器、蛍光シリカナノ粒子を用いた高感度検査キット）、インフラ維持管理、エアロスペースなどへ4コア技術を応用。』"}}}, text:
The AI Brain – 10 Infrastructure Layers related to neuroscience 
🧠 

1. Power & Electricity ⚡️ 
Neuroscience: Brainstem + Heart & Blood Vessels
AI infra: Power plants, electrical grids, substations
- This is the “blood” that keeps everything alive. AI needs huge amounts of electricity. No power = brain is dead

2. Cooling Systems 🧊 
Neuroscience: Body temperature control + Blood cooling
AI infra: Liquid cooling, big air conditioners, chillers
- AI chips get extremely hot, similar to running a marathon. Cooling stops the brain from overheating and breaking.

3. Datacenter Buildings 🏗️
Neuroscience: Skull + Outer Cerebral Cortex
AI infra: Huge warehouses full of server racks
- These are the “skull and brain tissue.” Big empty buildings that will later be filled. 

4. Compute Chips (GPUs & Accelerators) 💾
Neuroscience: Billions of Neurons (brain cells)
AI infra: The actual GPU chips and AI processors
- These are the individual “brain cells.” They do the heavy thinking and math for AI.

5. High-Speed Connections 🔌
Neuroscience: White Matter (nerve highways) + Corpus Callosum
AI infra: Fiber optic cables, networking switches, photonics
- Super-fast roads that let different parts of the brain talk to each other instantly.

6. Data Storage 💽
Anatomy: Hippocampus (memory center)
AI infra: Hard drives, SSDs, cloud storage systems
- This is where the brain keeps all its memories and knowledge safe for a long time.

7. Cloud & Neocloud Platforms 🧠☁️
Neuroscience: Neocortex (higher thinking areas)
AI infra: Companies like Nebius, CoreWeave, Crusoe, Lambda that rent out GPUs
- The “managers” that organize all the hardware and let people use the brain without owning it.

8. AI Software Frameworks 👨🏼‍💻
Neuroscience: Association areas (connecting ideas)
AI infra: Tools like PyTorch, Kubernetes, TensorFlow
- The easy-to-use “instruction manuals” that help people build and run AI on the hardware.

9. AI Models 🤓
Neuroscience: Prefrontal Cortex (planning & intelligence)
AI infra: Big models like GPT, Claude, Grok, Anthropic, etc.
- This is the actual “brain power” – the part that understands language, writes, codes, and reasons.

10. AI Agents & Robotics 🤖 
Neuroscience: Motor Cortex + Hands & Body (Peripheral Nervous System)
AI infra: AI agents, robots, self-driving cars, smart apps that take action
- This is the “body and hands.” It turns thinking into real-world actions.

text:
$MRVL is becoming one of the most important names in the AI infrastructure buildout.

Nvidia’s Jensen Huang publicly called Marvell the “next trillion-dollar company” at Computex, citing its role in solving data movement across massive GPU clusters.

UBS just lifted its price target to $340 (from $230), spotlighting Marvell’s leadership in CXL the technology that lets processors and memory share resources far more efficiently inside next-gen AI racks.

Add in the industry’s first 102.4 Tbps switch purpose-built for AI/cloud, a strategic $NVDA  NVLink Fusion partnership with a $2B Nvidia investment, and Marvell’s optical/photonics push via recent acquisitions.

Despite the recent semis pullback, the fundamentals and strategic positioning look increasingly durable.

$MRVL is a High-conviction AI connectivity play for the multi-year buildout 🔌

text:
Now is not a time to be focusing on anything crypto related imo. We are in the midst of building a brain for the AI infrastructure buildout that will shape our future and the next 2 decades 

It’s a time to focus on efficiency, energy, and productivity, which cryptocurrency has proven to be inversely correlated to in recent years 

🧠 
Neoclouds = the Neocortex of the brain 
Datacenters = the cerebral cortex and cortical columns 
Power & electrical grids = brainstem and circulatory system
Cooling systems = thermoregulation & cerebral blood flow 
Fiber optics & photonics = white matter & corpus callosum
Data storage = hippocampus and long term memory 
Compute & robotics = cerebellum, PNS, and motor cortex
AI agents = prefrontal cortex and “consciousness”

{"from_index": 1261, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 1272}, {"from_index": 1370, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 1376}]}, "text": "Tech Parallels: Dotcom bubble & AI bubble Datacenters and neoclouds to AI infrastructure are what servers, networking gear, and early datacenters are to the dotcom/internet boom of the 1990-2000s - Computers became the essential “picks and shovels” that enabled everything that followed from e-commerce, search, social media, mobile apps, and eventually cloud computing Essentially, datacenters are the “new computers” that run AI chips or GPUs/accelerators Parallel Layers: 1. Infrastructure/Compute Layer (“new computers”): - Dotcom era: Servers + internet backbone/fiber. - AI era: AI-optimized datacenters + accelerators + neoclouds/hyperscalers - Key difference in scale/intensity: AI workloads are extraordinarily power-hungry. U.S. data center power demand is projected to roughly double from ~31 GW in 2025 to ~66 GW by 2027 - globally on track to double or more by 2030, with AI-driven accelerated servers growing ~30% annually This creates new bottlenecks in power generation, grids, and cooling (hence the shift to liquid cooling) 2. Platform/Distribution Layer: Dotcom era: Early ISPs, web hosts, and eventually cloud (AWS launched 2006) AI era: Neoclouds + hyperscalers renting GPU clusters, plus orchestration tools 3. Application Layer: Dotcom era: After infrastructure matured post-bubble → social media, mobile apps, SaaS, e-commerce explosion AI era: Ongoing. The datacenter/neocloud buildout is laying the rails for the application layer - real AI mania comes from execution of AI agents, vertical AI tools, robotics/embodied intelligence, scientific discovery acceleration, and personalized everything in the future"}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2066033922803114355"}}, "rest_id": "2068390362515988966", "source": "Twitter for iPad", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2413", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2073492205583335483", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1312", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The real story behind Sivers’ capital raise + H1 earnings delay 

Last week, Sivers completed a directed share issue of 700 M SEK (up from the initial 600 M target).
 The deal was heavily oversubscribed → they increased the size. Issued at 57 SEK (only 9.7% discount).

This is positive news: fresh capital to scale InP laser production, R&D, and capture AI / LiDAR opportunities.

BUT as always , bears are screaming dilution and bad governance. 

The facts now : 

The controversial part: H1 earnings has been delayed (originally expected early August → end of Augus → end of August) + waiving the lock-up from the April raise.

What happened:
April: Small raise with 180-day lock-up.
Late June: Big 700 M SEK raise → Pareto waives the lock-up for April participants.
H1 results moved after lock-up expiry.

Bear interpretation :  Management is panicked, delaying results so insiders can sell before releasing bad numbers.
Like this , if the earning sucks , they will be able to sell before 

Bull / realistic interpretation:
A 700 M SEK oversubscribed raise by institutions doesn’t fit the narrative of a sinking ship.
New investors did their due diligence. They wouldn’t have joined if fundamentals were rotten. 
Let’s be real : they have info retail don’t ! You think they would go in if it was the case ? 

By waiving the lock-up, management shows strong confidence in the future: they’re not afraid investors will dump, because those investors believe in the long-term story (CPO, partnerships, capacity scaling). 

The earnings delay likely allows them to come out with stronger guidance and better visibility on 2027 ramps (CPO, POET, GF SCALE, etc.).

In summary ,  This is a volatile micro-cap in scaling mode. There will always be FUD on dilution, timing, etc.
But when institutions pile in on a 700 M SEK raise and management waives lock-ups without fear, it carries more weight than conspiracy theories about earnings timing.

Long term bullish 
Still holding 

#SIVE #Sivers #Photonics #CPO #AI #Nasdaq #Semiconductors #Stocks

I was scrolling trough my feed today, and the only thing I see is people comparing Quantum (2025) to photonics. 

Yeah we seen a very good run for quantum and then it just dumped. But I know why it dumped. Because THERE WAS NO REAL REVENUE. 

Comparing to photonics names like $AAOI they have real revenue and huge projections. So don’t compare some temporary bs to something real.

text:
$COHR $LITE $AAOI 

The recent hammering of photonics/CPO names, which was partly triggered by the SemiAnalysis note pushing timelines out for CPO adoption, looks like a short-sighted overreaction rather than a collapse in the fundamental thesis imo.

The realistic path is to use copper for scale-up as long as you can on short-reach, low-latency links inside the rack/cluster, and then move to optics as the final phase of the transition. The move to optics is fundamentally necessary. Copper interconnects eventually hit physical limits w/ exponentially rising power draw, signal degradation, and restricted reach as AI clusters scale to massive sizes w/ extreme bandwidth demands. Copper simply cannot efficiently move data across longer distances and at the densities required w/o consuming unsustainable power or compromising performance. 

This hybrid, phased reality is not a bear case. Copper staying relevant longer actually extends the runway and de-risks CPO adoption as clusters scale to a point where copper hits hard walls.

We’re still early in the multi-year AI infrastructure buildout. The “slow ramp” narrative today is exactly what you should be expect anyways before the volumes compound in the back half of the decade.

The “slow ramp” narrative today is exactly what you should be expect anyways before the volumes compound in the back half of the decade."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 1, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2072770760519925941", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "439", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Man, seeing semis get hammered again on what’s basically old Meta news is wild. I think Bloomberg just loves stirring the pot, UBS called it right, Zuckerberg talked about selling capacity ages ago. Those $48B neocloud deals don’t scream overbuild to me. I believe the real AI demand is still there, so I’m eyeing dips in $AAOI and $MRVL.

I’ve seen this movie before with the AMD/Nvidia export scare earlier this year. Markets overreact every time. I think this is weak hands folding while the big picture (power shortages, compute needs) hasn’t changed. Personally believe loading up on the AI supply chain names like $AEHR and photonics plays here could pay off big once the noise dies.

Honestly, semis trade on headlines more than reality short term. I believe this selloff is overdone, $META optimizing capacity isn’t the same as slashing AI spend. I think we’re getting a gift on $AAOI $SIVEF $LITE, $TER, etc. Staying long the thesis, volatility like this separates the believers from the rest.
@kaiseninvest Photonics cycle over aparantly.

text:
Dear @vonderleyen,
How wonderful that you and the entire College of Commissioners travelled to Cork today to visit the Tyndall National Institute! A real “innovation powerhouse,” as you so aptly put it. “From test to scale, from ideas to industry” – that sounds like proper European future music. And Ireland really is a beautiful country. The green hills, the Guinness, the friendly people… a business trip like that must be quite enjoyable. The European taxpayer is surely delighted that you’re all so motivated and on the road.
Just one small, modest question out of pure fiscal curiosity: Did you know that right on your doorstep in Leuven, Belgium, there is an institute that is considered the global benchmark in exactly this field – integrated semiconductors, nanoelectronics, photonics and deep-tech hardware?
imec employs over 6,500 scientists, engineers and innovators from more than 100 countries. That’s more than ten times the size of Tyndall (which had a proud 580 staff and students in 2024, including 173 postgraduates with 158 PhD students). imec was Belgium’s top patent applicant in 2024/2025 and is driving major European Chips Act pilot lines. While Tyndall is getting an expansion worth over €100 million and works with around 200 industry partners, imec has had a global network for years and generates well over a billion euros in revenue annually – mostly through direct industry collaborations.
But of course: why stay in Leuven when you can fly the whole College, security teams, protocol staff and all the rest to Ireland instead? The taxpayers in Germany, France, Italy and all the other member states are happy to pay for a bit of extra European unity – plus flights, hotels, escorted convoys and the entire logistical effort for a visit to Cork, when Leuven is only about a half-hour drive from Brussels.
We really do think it’s nice that you praise Tyndall so much. The institute has been doing good work since it was founded in 2004 (with roots in the NMRC from 1981). But if you’re going to celebrate European top-tier research, then please do it where it’s biggest, most established and closest. It would save money – and CO₂. The Irish government can still be happy anyway – they’ve already received plenty of EU funds.
With friendly but slightly puzzled regards,

Upcoming Events This Week - [July 6 - July 10, 2026]

Stay informed on events/news for the coming week.

Market Update

> 5 days into July, will July be a strong green month as it has been? 

> Memory, photonics, and now equipment has started to take a beating. Will this continue? Oh wait, SemiAnalysis came out with a negative read on Nvidia's Kyber, delayed till 2028 due to CPO challenges.

> SOX RSI at 34.70, closer to oversold than overbought. Typically a set up for another rally. 

Economic Calendar

> No one cares about ISM Services PMI, or Jobless Claims (unless shocking for the bad)

> Minutes of the Fed's June FOMC meeting on Wednesday, July 8th. Inflation risk is coming down significantly with oil prices back to where they were before the war BUT consumer electronics are seeing prices tick higher considerably. Is the Fed monitoring that?

text:
WPI’s Core Research Facilities provide shared access to advanced instrumentation—from nano-fabrication and microscopy to photonics, imaging, and medical device testing—powering research across many disciplines. 

Learn more about how you can become a part of our research community here: https://t.co/AhKDDoIZEx.

我写过 Soitec、SIVE、IQE、Shunsin、AAOI……很多人把它们当成各自独立的票。但如果你把这些环节摊在一张桌子上,会发现一个几乎没人问过的问题:它们最后,是在谁的工厂里被"连起来"造出来的?

今天我们来聊一下这家快被遗忘的欧洲小厂: $XFAB的故事。 先说几个我一直都觉得很有意思的点:

1. 首先它的出身就带有历史性意味，它是 1992 年从"东德解体后被拆分的电子工业巨头"里长出来的, 然后被一批比利时人接手, 而这批比利时人还顺手创办了另一家汽车芯片龙头 Melexis，这意味着同一批人,一手造芯片、一手造工厂。

2. 它不卷纳米, 却能够把英伟达和诺基亚拉进了自己的局。 它牵头的一个欧盟出钱的硅光子项目中英伟达、诺基亚、imec 都是同桌的参与方。 一家欧洲小代工厂,能召集起这种阵容,本身就说明问题。

3. 它可能是我这一整条硅光/CPO 链的"总枢纽"。 它在 Soitec 的 SOI 地基上、用 SMART Photonics 的 InP,造出喂给 AAOI 的硅光芯片;同时又是 NVTS 那条"电"线上的 SiC 代工龙头，一只手 AI 光、一只手 EV 电。 

但真正让我觉得很神奇的点在于它约六成的股份,锁在两个创始家族和一个州政府手里，但我们需要知道为什么是这样？而这样又意味着什么？

Soitec、SIVE、IQE、Shunsin、AAOI……很多人把它们当成各自独立的票。但如果你把这些环节摊在一张桌子上,会发现一个几乎没人问过的问题:它们最后,是在谁的工厂里被"连起来"造出来的? 今天我们来聊一下这家快被遗忘的欧洲小厂: $XFAB的故事。 先说几个我一直都觉得很有意思的点: 1. 首先它的出身就带有历史性意味，它是 1992 年从"东德解体后被拆分的电子工业巨头"里长出来的, 然后被一批比利时人接手, 而这批比利时人还顺手创办了另一家汽车芯片龙头 Melexis，这意味着同一批人,一手造芯片、一手造工厂。 2. 它不卷纳米, 却能够把英伟达和诺基亚拉进了自己的局。 它牵头的一个欧盟出钱的硅光子项目中英伟达、诺基亚、imec 都是同桌的参与方。 一家欧洲小代工厂,能召集起这种阵容,本身就说明问题。 3. 它可能是我这一整条硅光/CPO 链的"总枢纽"。 它在 Soitec 的 SOI 地基上、用 SMART Photonics 的 InP,造出喂给 AAOI 的硅光芯片;同时又是 NVTS 那条"电"线上的 SiC 代工龙头，一只手 AI 光、一只手 EV 电。 但真正让我觉得很神奇的点在于它约六成的股份,锁在两个创始家族和一个州政府手里，但我们需要知道为什么是这样？而这样又意味着什么？ text:
I'm seeing it every day. 

Investors comparing their YTD, YoY performance, and even weekly/daily performance. 

It's really not fair to compare apples and pumpkins. 

- Investor investing in cyclical companies will definitely go up and down based on the cycle and timing.
- Investor investing in high growth will perform during low rates but struggle during inflation.
- Investor investing in value will miss huge runs but they focus on safety.
- Investing in biotech, pharma, car, energy industry all brings different outcomes. If car industry is lagging, it's impossible to expect an SME for this industry to find 10 baggers in photonics. 

That's just one piece of the story. 

The next one is about your investing style or strategy.
- Long?
- Day/swing trading? 
- Using options? 
- Using all of the above? 

In a volatile environment swing trader should have more options and long investors may underperform. If the market has bearish outlook, shorters may have more luck than the rest?

We can't compare traders in robotics vs long term investors in consumer staples. Makes no sense. 

There's the last piece of the story. 

Not everyone is having the same amount of cash available, nor the same risk tolerance, nor the same time horizon, nor time commitment.  

One investor has a difficult work and no time to pay attention to daily movements. He can't really go and day trade or swing trade. 
Another one with 5 billion portfolio cannot have 50% in a single month. He is likely happy with 3%. 
A student having 200 usd to throw in $SIVE and getting 5x cannot be compared to someone managing his family portfolio. 

When you next time compare yourself with someone, make sure to understand these three dimensions. 

It's VERY likely the guy you're comparing yourself with has different investing profile and strategy. Maybe he isn't investing in memory and buying selling every day. 

Only compare your current performance to your own last month or last year. 

Competing with oneself is one of the most effective paths to sustained personal growth in anything you do.

TBB

text:
Mapping $SIVE 2030:

Current: ~52 SEK 

2030 Target: 260 SEK 

Cap: ~85B SEK

The catalyst is the AI Power Wall. As copper interconnects fail, Sivers photonics becomes the standard for data center light sources. A pure play on the transition from hardware to AI infrastructure.

For $SIVE, the 2030 re-rating is a bet on the "Optical Transition." Right now, the market sees them as a niche small-cap component maker. By 2030, they have the potential to be the standard-bearer for light-source technology in AI clusters.
Here is why I believe the bull case for a ~260 SEK target is realistic:

1.The Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Shift: Data centers are hitting a "Power Wall." Traditional copper wires generate too much heat and lose too much signal at AI scale. Moving data with light (photonics) is 1,000x more efficient. Sivers Photonics produces the high-power DFB lasers that act as the "engine" for these optical interconnects.

2.The LEO Satellite Explosion: Beyond AI, their beamforming chips are core to the Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite market. As global internet coverage shifts to space, the demand for low-cost, high-efficiency ground and satellite terminals will move from thousands to millions of units.

3.The 6G Infrastructure Bridge: As we move toward 2030, the 6G standard will rely heavily on mmWave technology. Sivers has been refining this tech for years while others were focused only on standard 5G. They own the "hard part" of the spectrum.
If they successfully navigate the liquidity hurdles of the next few years, the "massive gap" you see is the market finally pricing them as a critical infrastructure provider rather than a speculative hardware play.

text:
Mapping the 2030 landscape: the gap between current prices and future market caps for $AAOI, $MU, and $SNDK is massive.

$AAOI: $135 to $1,200 

$MU: $1,060 to $3,200 

$SNDK: $1,880 to $4,800

The tech backbone is being fundamentally re-valued. This is asymmetry in action.

The "Why" behind the 2030 targets:

1.$AAOI (The Photonics Pivot): The move from 400G to 800G and eventually 1.6T connectivity is the massive catalyst here. As AI clusters grow, the "plumbing" (optical transceivers) becomes the primary bottleneck. We are looking at a fundamental re-rating from a hardware supplier to a critical AI infrastructure provider.

2.$MU (The HBM Standard): High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) has changed the game. Memory is no longer a cyclical commodity; it's a dedicated AI component with much higher margins. By 2030, $MU won't just be selling chips, they will be selling the "oxygen" that keeps AI GPUs running.

3.$SNDK (Enterprise Data Lakes): As companies move from training AI to running inference on massive datasets, the demand for enterprise-grade NAND (storage) will explode. $SNDK is positioned to capture the shift toward high-density, low-power storage required for 2030-era data centers.

text:
No LN₂ required. More uptime. Easier maintenance.

For semiconductor failure analysis, eliminating liquid nitrogen isn't just about convenience—it means simplified maintenance, greater system uptime, and more consistent operation.

Introducing the new Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera.

When integrated with our inverted emission microscope, the Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera featuring innovative Solid Cold (linear cooling) technology delivers stable, 24-hour photoemission analysis without the need for LN₂ cooling. Combined with a wide field of view, a high signal-to-noise sensor, and high-speed wavelength switching, it enables highly sensitive detection of weak photon emissions.

Available now. 👉 Learn more: https://t.co/kMak30KFLi

Attending IPFA 2026 in Singapore, July 13–16? Stop by our booth to see the new Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera and connect with our team.

#Semiconductor #FailureAnalysis #Photonics #SemiconductorManufacturing #Engineering

text:
If you build it they won't come!

"...The TFLN platform incorporates topological photonic crystals to create protected edge-state waveguides. These structures provide backscattering-immune light propagation that is inherently robust against defects and disorder (as demonstrated in topological photonics literature). This physical-layer protection complements the system-level topological surplus generated via Artin braid inversion, resulting in multi-layered resilience."

Market Review (Mid-Day) 20260707:
- Semi and Memory washout this morning, BUT still trying to hang in there. Basically all the prior hardware leading sectors, including Photonics, Data Centres etc.
- I've been banging the drum about money rotating OUT of AI HARDWARE for about 2 weeks now. So this is no surprise. The AI Trade is NOT over. I just see money rotating now to the next layer. 
- Where? Remember I talked about Cybersecurity and selected software? It is becoming clearer that it is mostly storage and database management related. That is why DELL, HPE, NTAP, SNOW, DDOG, RBRK, all continue to do well. These guys STORE and MANAGE the Data. And then we have CRWD PANW FTNT. These guys PROTECT the data. RDDT seems to be ok too. They have a lot of data. 
- See what is the common denominator? DATA. At the end of the day, AI needs data to function to its best ability. And that data needs to be stored and managed properly. 
- There is another layer on top of the Data layer, which is usage / application. Biotech is a clear beneficiary and that's why its been leading recently. Fintech seems to take to it easily as well, and has been doing well too. 
- Interestingly, one of my favourite companies at the moment, ZETA, play both in the DATA and benefits from USAGE. But it’s a very volatile small cap. Not for everyone. 
- So, what now?
- I don’t know your investment horizon. If long enough, the AI hardware trade I think will come back at some point. It needs to base and rest and for now. Just beware that often, distribution / topping sometimes looks like basing / resting. We only know after the fact. So we have to bear that in mind as a possible scenario. What I am saying is that, using Memory as an example, it is POSSIBLE that it has topped. I do not know and don't want to call it, but I have it at the back of my mind as a POSSIBILITY. MU, 10X in a year. What more do you want? Yeah, I know it has low PE, forward orders blah blah blah. They all look like that at tops. The most important question is, WHO ELSE is out there who needs to buy that hasn’t already bought? 
- So, instead of continuing to buy / hold something that has POTENTIALLY topped, why not buy something else which has a higher probability of continuing the uptrend? Which is why the portfolio started to shift about 2-3 weeks ago into these other stocks. You will notice it if you have been copying my port for a while. In Apr and May, we were full of semiconductors, memory, photonics etc. Software in late May. And since then mainly the themes I talked about above. 
- Other themes that are kindda standing out are consumer staples, quality stocks, financials, retail. This is why I am not totally bearish on the market overall. Market is just rotating to other sectors at the moment, NOT leaving the market totally. I don't see outright panic selling (though you might feel differently if you are holding all the previously hot stocks like AMD, INTC, AMAT, SNDK, MU, WDC, ALAB, MRVL, AAOI, LITE). 
- One small tell is worth mentioning. WULF announced a major deal with Anthropic yesterday and gapped up at the open by about 17%? It sold off through the day, and today took out the deal announcement low as well as the prior day low! News and REACTION like this is very important for me. Remember I talked about how WDC could not hold the good news from MU? That was one canary in the coal mine. Then MU could not hold. Then Samsung. And WULF. ALL the hardware names simply could not catch a bid from good news. And if stocks cannot go up on good news, what do you expect them to do? There's only one other direction from there…
- So, for me, I am avoiding most of the AI hardware players for now. And focusing on the DATABASE and SECURITY related players going forward. 
- Just my 2 cents. Could be wrong. DYOR. NFA.

text:
Chip-Scale Ultrafast Lasers Could Bring Femtosecond Performance to Portable Photonics

https://t.co/vl1knOZqWI 

#ChipScale #UltraFastLasers #Femtosecond #PortablePhotonics #RPMCLasers https://t.co/6vgXBgACDI

A Small Business Today, But Management's Ambition Is Much Bigger

At the moment, IzmoMicro is still a relatively small business. It generated around ₹18-19 crore of revenue in FY26, but management expects this to increase to ₹45-50 crore in FY27.

More importantly, the building blocks are gradually falling into place. The company already has an order book of around ₹40 crore, a pipeline of nearly ₹100 crore, partnerships with IIT Madras, Alcyon Photonics and CCRAFT, and has established a presence in Germany to access European semiconductor customers. 

Management's long-term aspiration is to build IzmoMicro into a ₹1,200-1,500 crore business over the next five years.

text:
So Where Does IZMO Fit In?

This is where many investors misunderstand the story.
IZMO is not trying to manufacture AI chips. Instead, it is building one of the most critical parts that allows those chips to work efficiently - advanced semiconductor packaging and silicon photonics packaging. 

In simple words, even the most powerful chip is of little use if it cannot communicate quickly with the rest of the system.

What's interesting is that as semiconductor technology becomes more complex, packaging itself is becoming increasingly valuable. Management shared that while traditional packaging contributes only 8-10% of a chip's value, advanced packaging contributes around 50%, and silicon photonics packaging can account for almost 80%. That is a remarkable shift in where value is being created.

text:
The Biggest AI Opportunity Isn't Always the Chip

Whenever AI is discussed, companies like NVIDIA usually dominate the conversation. Everyone wants faster GPUs, more computing power and larger AI models.

But there's another problem quietly becoming just as important - moving data between those chips. As AI models become larger, the amount of information flowing inside data centres is exploding. 

Traditional copper connections are slowly reaching their limits, and that's where the industry is turning towards silicon photonics, which uses light instead of electricity to move data much faster.

text:
IZMO: The ₹50 Crore Business That Could Become a ₹1,500 Crore Opportunity

IZMO is no longer just an automotive software company. It is quietly building a semiconductor packaging business that management believes can grow from ~₹50 crore in FY27 to ₹1,200-1,500 crore over the next five years.

Here's everything you need to know about IzmoMicro, silicon photonics, advanced packaging, and why management believes this could become the company's biggest growth engine. 🧵👇

text:
AMD is not alone or necessarily the first to market in an exclusive way, but they are very much in the race. Ayar Labs has secured major investments, with AMD Ventures, NVIDIA, and Intel Capital all contributing heavy multi-million dollar sums. All three giants are after the same thing: replacing traditional copper cables inside and between AI processors with light signals (optical I/O) to build ultra-fast AI supercomputers.

2. What is SuperNova?

SuperNova is an external light source (a type of multi-laser device) that sends light across up to 16 different wavelengths through thin fiber-optic cables. Instead of sending electricity through copper, data is transmitted at the speed of light. This provides vastly higher bandwidth, lower latency, and consumes significantly less power, which is exactly what future, massive AI models require.

3. The Sivers Connection

There is actually an interesting Swedish angle here: it is the semiconductor company Sivers Semiconductors through its subsidiary Sivers Photonics that has developed and delivered the advanced laser arrays used inside Ayar Labs' SuperNova modules.

AMD is at the absolute forefront, integrating this technology into its future chip designs, but they share the pie and the technology with their biggest competitors Nvidia and Intel, who are also deeply involved with Ayar Labs.

text:
Another example that it’s not just $SIVE

The whole AI trade has been hit very hard recently, photonics especially.

We’re seeing drops everywhere. The  broader market is in drawdown and fear mode.

If you have a thesis and nothing has changed then let it play out and don’t panic.

I’m still long on $SIVE and buying more whenever I can.
embedded_text:
$AAOI has taken a massive hit.

The share price dropped from $223 to $113. That is a 49% drop from its recent high.

I thought I sold too early at $167, but this is a good reminder that no stock goes up in a straight line. https://t.co/AgeIjkpVPJ

text: Yesterday marked six years since Intel was granted the patent for its method of integrating waveguides into the substrate, a technology that, alongside others advances in photonics, will lead to the emergence of photonic Network-on-Chip XPUs in the near future.
embedded_text:
Gradually, more patents are emerging which may lead to the development of an Intel photonic NoC. Here is one more of them.

Patent: Substrate integrated waveguide - Intel

More details: https://t.co/F7K56QCdCE https://t.co/oKhcRvWCzN

text:
Let me break it down for you, my friend, it's very simple

Do you know why it has been so abrupt and silent?

Because they caused it, because for the first time in history, retail investors got ahead of the institutions

And they are furious

That's why they have "collapsed" the market using nothing but reports and phrases taken out of context, or literal lies, just to tank it

They are breaking a narrative that remains completely intact and is improving at a breakneck pace

Now, why is it the most silent one?

Because it doesn't suit them to talk about it

They are loading up like crazy while retail sells like crazy because "it's the end”

It’s been seen in many names, I'll give you $SIVE as an example

The transfer from Swedish Retail -> US Retail is wrapping up

And coincidentally, we start seeing the first buys from institutions like JP Morgan

What happened just a few days later?

$SIVE drops 65%

Weird, isn't it? and nobody is talking about it, wonder why?

They want your shares, and they are getting them

Don't be surprised if for the rest of the month all you see are bullish articles reinforcing AI narratives, from memory to photonics and everything in between

And obviously, their multi million dollar positions in all these names will come to light

Don't have a single doubt about it

Or is this simultaneously the most brutal but also silent sell off ever?

I haven't read a SINGLE article from the mainstream media about this steep crash with many popular stocks down 30-50% from their highs.

Are retail investors in a private hell?

text:

🔬 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Stephen Hawking predicted that black holes slowly evaporate by emitting radiation — but testing this in space remains impossible. Now, physicists have experimentally studied the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a lab-based system mimicking a black hole's event horizon with light and fiber optics. This is a landmark step: not just detecting analogue Hawking radiation, but observing how it *affects the system that produced it*. Understanding backreaction is crucial to resolving one of physics' deepest puzzles — the black hole information paradox. Lab-scale black hole mimics are becoming powerful tools for probing quantum gravity.

#HawkingRadiation #QuantumPhysics #BlackHoles #AnalogueGravity #QuantumOptics

text:
For years, the conversation around AI has centered on faster GPUs and larger models.

But as AI infrastructure scales, a different challenge is emerging.

Moving massive amounts of data between GPUs requires greater bandwidth, lower latency, improved power efficiency, and architectures designed to scale.

Kopin, together with FabricAI, is developing Neural I/o™, a MicroLED-based optical interconnect technology designed to enable faster, lower-power communication for the next generation of AI infrastructure.

The companies leading the next era of AI won't just build smarter models. They'll build smarter infrastructure, and Kopin is helping to make that future possible.

Learn more: https://t.co/toXxjRb7CO

#KopinTech #ArtificialIntelligence #AIInfrastructure #Photonics #MicroLED #MadeInUSA @FabricAIFABC

text:
Wieczorne podsumowanie rynków | 08.07.2026 📈📉

Środa była dniem powrotu geopolityki na pierwszy plan. Donald Trump ogłosił, że porozumienie z Iranem jest skończone, ropa wystrzeliła, rentowności amerykańskich obligacji poszły w górę, a inwestorzy znów zaczęli liczyć potencjalne koszty konfliktu w rejonie Cieśniny Ormuz 🛢️⚠️.

Na tym tle GPW wypadła zaskakująco dobrze. WIG20 w ciągu dnia tracił prawie 2%, ale finalnie zakończył sesję niemal na zero. Warszawa dostała cios z zewnątrz, ale nie położyła się na deskach 🇵🇱🥊.

🇵🇱 GPW: Duża nerwowość, mała zmiana na zamknięciu 🦅
Na zamknięciu WIG20 spadł symbolicznie o 0,05%, szeroki WIG stracił 0,06%, a sWIG80 zniżkował o 0,47%. Najlepiej zachował się mWIG40, który wzrósł o 0,23%.

Obroty były wysokie i wyniosły 2,45 mld zł, z czego ponad 2 mld zł przypadło na WIG20. Największy handel koncentrował się na KGHM, Allegro, Orlenie i PKO BP. To była sesja dużych spółek i dużych emocji 💰.

Orlen korzysta z drogiej ropy: Liderem WIG20 został Orlen (+3,51%). Skok cen ropy natychmiast poprawił sentyment do paliwowego giganta. W tle inwestorzy wyceniali ryzyko kolejnych problemów z transportem surowca przez Cieśninę Ormuz ⛽📈.

Banki pomagają indeksowi: Dobrze wyglądały też Alior (+1,38%), Pekao (+1,34%) i PKO BP (+1,22%). To ważne, bo dziś RPP zgodnie z oczekiwaniami utrzymała stopy procentowe bez zmian, ze stopą referencyjną na poziomie 3,75%. Rynek dostał więc pauzę, a nie zaskoczenie 🏦.

Allegro i Dino po zielonej stronie: W drugiej części sesji popyt pojawił się także na Allegro (+0,76%) i Dino (+0,80%). To pomogło WIG20 ograniczyć skalę spadku, mimo mocnej przeceny kilku ciężkich komponentów indeksu 🛒.

KGHM głównym hamulcem: Największym problemem GPW był dziś KGHM (-6,69%). Spółce ciążyły spadki miedzi i srebra, a inwestorzy nadal trawią nową strategię 2055+. Rynek coraz uważniej patrzy na możliwe wyższe nakłady inwestycyjne i potencjalne transakcje M&A ⛏️📉.

Budimex i przemysł pod presją: Słabo wypadł Budimex (-3,70%), a pod kreską znalazły się też Kęty (-1,74%) i Modivo (-1,49%). Bez wsparcia Orlenu i banków WIG20 wyglądałby dziś znacznie gorzej.

Szeroki rynek z kilkoma mocnymi historiami: W mWIG40 świetnie poradziły sobie Inter Cars (+5,50%), Creotech (+4,56%) i Text (+4,21%). Creotech pomógł pozytywny komunikat o uzyskaniu łączności z satelitą Mikroglob-1, a Text kontynuował dobrą passę po wcześniejszych pozytywnych impulsach analitycznych 🚀.

Po słabszej stronie znalazły się Polimex Mostostal (-4,96%), Lubawa (-3,94%), Rainbow (-3,66%) i Ten Square Games (-3,50%). W sWIG80 uwagę przyciągał Huuuge (-3,64%) po słabszych danych o przychodach za II kwartał, a pozytywnie wyróżniło się VIGO Photonics (+1,85%) po rekordowych kwartalnych przychodach.

🇺🇸 Wall Street: Ropa straszy Dow, chipy ratują Nasdaq 🇺🇸
Za oceanem nastroje były nerwowe. Dow Jones tracił ponad 1%, S&P 500 był pod kreską, a Nasdaq zdołał odrobić część strat i krążył blisko poziomu odniesienia. To pokazuje, że rynek nie sprzedawał wszystkiego po równo. Największy ciężar spadł na sektory wrażliwe na wzrost cen ropy i rentowności.

Przemysł i materiały pod presją: Najsłabiej wyglądały spółki przemysłowe i materiałowe. Droższa ropa to wyższe koszty transportu, produkcji i finansowania, więc inwestorzy szybko zaczęli ciąć ekspozycję na bardziej cykliczne segmenty rynku 🏭📉.

Linie lotnicze i rejsy dostają cios: Wzrost cen paliwa uderzył też w spółki turystyczne i transportowe. United Airlines i Delta były pod presją, a operatorzy rejsów, tacy jak Carnival i Norwegian Cruise Line, również mocno traciły ✈️🚢.

Chipy po lepszej stronie rynku: Ciekawie wyglądały półprzewodniki. Broadcom mocno rósł po informacji, że Apple planuje wydać ponad 30 mld USD w ramach umowy dotyczącej dostaw chipów. Nvidia również była na plusie po doniesieniach, że Chiny mogą dopuścić ograniczone zakupy chipów H200 przez największe lokalne firmy AI 🤖💻.

To ważna zmiana po ostatnich dniach nerwowości. AI trade nadal jest chwiejny, ale nie został porzucony. Rynek po prostu coraz ostrzej rozróżnia spółki z konkretnymi kontraktami od tych, które rosną głównie na narracji.

🌍 Europa: Czerwień na Starym Kontynencie 🇪🇺
Europa zareagowała na eskalację znacznie słabiej niż GPW. W momencie zamknięcia handlu w Warszawie niemiecki DAX tracił ponad 2%, podobnie słabo wyglądał francuski CAC 40, a brytyjski FTSE 100 spadał o ok. 1,7%.

Warszawa na tym tle była relatywnie mocna. Pomogła struktura WIG20: drożejący Orlen, odbicie banków i popyt na kilka dużych spółek zrównoważyły mocny spadek KGHM. W Europie takiego bufora było mniej 🇪🇺⚖️.

💱 Waluty, obligacje i Fed: Ropa podbija inflacyjne obawy 💵
Na rynku długu zrobiło się nerwowo. Rentowność amerykańskich 10-latek wzrosła w okolice 4,56%, a 30-latek przekroczyła 5%. To reakcja na drożejącą ropę i obawy, że kolejny szok energetyczny może utrudnić Fedowi walkę z inflacją.

Do tego doszły minutes z czerwcowego posiedzenia Fedu. Zapiski pokazały podzielony obraz: część członków widzi argumenty za podwyżką stóp, choć na ostatnim posiedzeniu poparła jeszcze utrzymanie ich bez zmian. Rynek dostał więc sygnał, że Fed pod wodzą Kevina Warsha nie zamierza szybko wracać do luźniejszej polityki 🏦.

Dolar zyskiwał na statusie bezpiecznej przystani, a złoty pozostawał pod presją globalnego risk-off. W kraju głównym wydarzeniem była decyzja RPP, ale jej wpływ był ograniczony, bo brak zmiany stóp był szeroko oczekiwany.

🛢️ Ropa: Geopolityczna premia wraca z impetem ⛽
Najważniejszy ruch dnia wydarzył się na ropie. Brent rósł w okolice 78-80 USD za baryłkę, a WTI przekraczała 73 USD. Rynek wyceniał ryzyko dalszej eskalacji po amerykańskich uderzeniach na Iran i wypowiedziach Trumpa o końcu porozumienia.

Dodatkowym czynnikiem było cofnięcie licencji pozwalającej Iranowi na globalny eksport ropy. To zwiększyło obawy o dostępność surowca i przypomniało inwestorom, jak wrażliwy pozostaje rynek energii na każdy sygnał z Bliskiego Wschodu 🕊️⚠️.

💡 Wniosek dnia:
Środowa sesja była lekcją odporności GPW. Globalnie dominował strach przed eskalacją konfliktu USA-Iran, skokiem cen ropy i powrotem inflacyjnej presji. Europa mocno traciła, Wall Street była pod presją, a rentowności obligacji rosły.

Na tym tle Warszawa zachowała się bardzo solidnie. WIG20 niemal odrobił silną poranną przecenę dzięki Orlenowi, bankom, Allegro i Dino. Z drugiej strony tak głęboki spadek KGHM pokazuje, że rynek pozostaje bardzo selektywny i karze spółki, przy których pojawiają się jednocześnie presja surowcowa oraz obawy o przyszłe wydatki inwestycyjne.

Teraz najważniejsze będą trzy pytania: czy eskalacja wokół Iranu okaże się tylko kolejnym epizodem, czy początkiem większego kryzysu; czy ropa utrzyma się przy wyższych poziomach; oraz jak rynek zinterpretuje bardziej jastrzębie sygnały z Fedu. Jeśli ropa dalej będzie rosła, temat inflacji bardzo szybko wróci na pierwsze strony rynkowej agendy.

To pomogło WIG20 ograniczyć skalę spadku, mimo mocnej przeceny kilku ciężkich komponentów indeksu 🛒. KGHM głównym hamulcem: Największym problemem GPW był dziś KGHM (-6,69%). Spółce ciążyły spadki miedzi i srebra, a inwestorzy nadal trawią nową strategię 2055+. Rynek coraz uważniej patrzy na możliwe wyższe nakłady inwestycyjne i potencjalne transakcje M&A ⛏️📉. Budimex i przemysł pod presją: Słabo wypadł Budimex (-3,70%), a pod kreską znalazły się też Kęty (-1,74%) i Modivo (-1,49%). Bez wsparcia Orlenu i banków WIG20 wyglądałby dziś znacznie gorzej. Szeroki rynek z kilkoma mocnymi historiami: W mWIG40 świetnie poradziły sobie Inter Cars (+5,50%), Creotech (+4,56%) i Text (+4,21%). Creotech pomógł pozytywny komunikat o uzyskaniu łączności z satelitą Mikroglob-1, a Text kontynuował dobrą passę po wcześniejszych pozytywnych impulsach analitycznych 🚀. Po słabszej stronie znalazły się Polimex Mostostal (-4,96%), Lubawa (-3,94%), Rainbow (-3,66%) i Ten Square Games (-3,50%). W sWIG80 uwagę przyciągał Huuuge (-3,64%) po słabszych danych o przychodach za II kwartał, a pozytywnie wyróżniło się VIGO Photonics (+1,85%) po rekordowych kwartalnych przychodach. 🇺🇸 Wall Street: Ropa straszy Dow, chipy ratują Nasdaq 🇺🇸 Za oceanem nastroje były nerwowe. Dow Jones tracił ponad 1%, S&P 500 był pod kreską, a Nasdaq zdołał odrobić część strat i krążył blisko poziomu odniesienia. To pokazuje, że rynek nie sprzedawał wszystkiego po równo. Największy ciężar spadł na sektory wrażliwe na wzrost cen ropy i rentowności. Przemysł i materiały pod presją: Najsłabiej wyglądały spółki przemysłowe i materiałowe. Droższa ropa to wyższe koszty transportu, produkcji i finansowania, więc inwestorzy szybko zaczęli ciąć ekspozycję na bardziej cykliczne segmenty rynku 🏭📉. Linie lotnicze i rejsy dostają cios: Wzrost cen paliwa uderzył też w spółki turystyczne i transportowe. United Airlines i Delta były pod presją, a operatorzy rejsów, tacy jak Carnival i Norwegian Cruise Line, również mocno traciły ✈️🚢. Chipy po lepszej stronie rynku: Ciekawie wyglądały półprzewodniki. Broadcom mocno rósł po informacji, że Apple planuje wydać ponad 30 mld USD w ramach umowy dotyczącej dostaw chipów. Nvidia również była na plusie po doniesieniach, że Chiny mogą dopuścić ograniczone zakupy chipów H200 przez największe lokalne firmy AI 🤖💻. To ważna zmiana po ostatnich dniach nerwowości. AI trade nadal jest chwiejny, ale nie został porzucony. Rynek po prostu coraz ostrzej rozróżnia spółki z konkretnymi kontraktami od tych, które rosną głównie na narracji. 🌍 Europa: Czerwień na Starym Kontynencie 🇪🇺 Europa zareagowała na eskalację znacznie słabiej niż GPW. W momencie zamknięcia handlu w Warszawie niemiecki DAX tracił ponad 2%, podobnie słabo wyglądał francuski CAC 40, a brytyjski FTSE 100 spadał o ok. 1,7%. Warszawa na tym tle była relatywnie mocna. Pomogła struktura WIG20: drożejący Orlen, odbicie banków i popyt na kilka dużych spółek zrównoważyły mocny spadek KGHM. W Europie takiego bufora było mniej 🇪🇺⚖️. 💱 Waluty, obligacje i Fed: Ropa podbija inflacyjne obawy 💵 Na rynku długu zrobiło się nerwowo. Rentowność amerykańskich 10-latek wzrosła w okolice 4,56%, a 30-latek przekroczyła 5%. To reakcja na drożejącą ropę i obawy, że kolejny szok energetyczny może utrudnić Fedowi walkę z inflacją. Do tego doszły minutes z czerwcowego posiedzenia Fedu. Zapiski pokazały podzielony obraz: część członków widzi argumenty za podwyżką stóp, choć na ostatnim posiedzeniu poparła jeszcze utrzymanie ich bez zmian. Rynek dostał więc sygnał, że Fed pod wodzą Kevina Warsha nie zamierza szybko wracać do luźniejszej polityki 🏦. Dolar zyskiwał na statusie bezpiecznej przystani, a złoty pozostawał pod presją globalnego risk-off. W kraju głównym wydarzeniem była decyzja RPP, ale jej wpływ był ograniczony, bo brak zmiany stóp był szeroko oczekiwany. 🛢️ Ropa: Geopolityczna premia wraca z impetem ⛽ Najważniejszy ruch dnia wydarzył się na ropie. Brent rósł w okolice 78-80 USD za baryłkę, a WTI przekraczała 73 USD. Rynek wyceniał ryzyko dalszej eskalacji po amerykańskich uderzeniach na Iran i wypowiedziach Trumpa o końcu porozumienia. Dodatkowym czynnikiem było cofnięcie licencji pozwalającej Iranowi na globalny eksport ropy. To zwiększyło obawy o dostępność surowca i przypomniało inwestorom, jak wrażliwy pozostaje rynek energii na każdy sygnał z Bliskiego Wschodu 🕊️⚠️. 💡 Wniosek dnia: Środowa sesja była lekcją odporności GPW. Globalnie dominował strach przed eskalacją konfliktu USA-Iran, skokiem cen ropy i powrotem inflacyjnej presji. Europa mocno traciła, Wall Street była pod presją, a rentowności obligacji rosły. Na tym tle Warszawa zachowała się bardzo solidnie. WIG20 niemal odrobił silną poranną przecenę dzięki Orlenowi, bankom, Allegro i Dino. Z drugiej strony tak głęboki spadek KGHM pokazuje, że rynek pozostaje bardzo selektywny i karze spółki, przy których pojawiają się jednocześnie presja surowcowa oraz obawy o przyszłe wydatki inwestycyjne. Teraz najważniejsze będą trzy pytania: czy eskalacja wokół Iranu okaże się tylko kolejnym epizodem, czy początkiem większego kryzysu; czy ropa utrzyma się przy wyższych poziomach; oraz jak rynek zinterpretuje bardziej jastrzębie sygnały z Fedu. Jeśli ropa dalej będzie rosła, temat inflacji bardzo szybko wróci na pierwsze strony rynkowej agendy. #Giełda #WIG20 #GPW #WallStreet #Fed #Iran #Broadcom #Nvidia #FinTwitt #Inwestowanie"}}}, "rest_id": "2074934983177785689", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "823", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Become an IEEE Photonics Society Distinguished Lecturer

Applications are now open for the 2027 IEEE Photonics Society Distinguished Lecturer Program.

This prestigious program recognizes outstanding speakers who have made significant technical, industrial, or entrepreneurial contributions to the field of photonics. Distinguished Lecturers share their expertise with IEEE Photonics Society Chapters worldwide, helping inspire and educate the global photonics community.

Selected lecturers receive travel support and commit to presenting a minimum of six chapter lectures during their term.

Application package includes:
• Research accomplishments and invited talks
• Three-page CV
• 2–3 endorsement letters
• Technical and career-focused presentation abstracts
• Technical presentation video

📅 Application Deadline: 31 August 2026
Apply today and help shape the future of photonics education and outreach.

🔗 Submit your application: https://t.co/jc8W5BtBId

#IEEEPhotonics #Photonics #Engineering #Research #STEM #ProfessionalDevelopment #AcademicLeadership

text:
Excellent thread from @crux_capital_ on photonics investing.
Most people focus on the risks laser oversupply or a shift toward VCSELs over Silicon Photonics InP.
But the sharper angle is asking who actually benefits from cheaper more available InP, or which companies can capture value no matter which architecture wins.
Hyperscalers building AI data centers win from lower component costs.
System integrators and flexible players (think $SIVE $CIEN ) thrive because they integrate whatever lasers perform best.
Diversified names like $COHR and $LITE or $AAOI are also well positioned across techs.
It’s really about durability and resilience in the AI driven optical supercycle, not just short-term valuations or sector sentiment.
Smart way to think about it which companies stand out to you as true beneficiaries here?

And that's good and productive. And very nuanced. and you will go down a deep rabbit hole if you really want a solid answer. But there is another angle that I think is underappreciated. > Who BENEFITS FROM the oversupply? > Which companies would love for InP to be more available? > Or which companies capture more dollars REGARDLESS of the architecture used or who the laser vendors are. Things to think about... Because regardless of whatever the general headwind sentiment is for this industry, there are companies that will outperform. This is separate from valuations. text:
Some thoughts on Photonics...
$SIVE $CIEN $AAOI $COHR $LITE etc.

Whether the risk is laser oversupply
Or VSCELs used for scale up over SiPh

The first train of thought usually goes to... who is directly exposed to these risks? 

> Who is banking on a continued supply/demand imbalance?

> or if your building out massive InP supply for scale up and the market decides to prioritize VSCELs, then what?

And that's good and productive. And very nuanced. and you will go down a deep rabbit hole if you really want a solid answer. 

But there is another angle that I think is underappreciated.

> Who BENEFITS FROM the oversupply?

> Which companies would love for InP to be more available? 

> Or which companies capture more dollars REGARDLESS of the architecture used or who the laser vendors are.

Things to think about...

Because regardless of whatever the general headwind sentiment is for this industry, there are companies that will outperform.

This is separate from valuations. The market can decide that it wants to compress the multiples across all 'optics' companies regardless.

This is about company durability.

text:
Market Review 20260702
- "knock knock"
- Friend sent me this picture today, and it captures what I've been saying in recent posts so well. The sector rotation is real. But first, the overall market is actually doing ok, for now. Cautiously optimistic but highly aware it could break down easily.
- Top down, SPX is technically still in a uptrend but I don't like the fact that momentum in the higher timeframe has slowed down dramatically and might potentially signal the start of a downtrend in the lower timeframe, the daily.
- Instead of choosing red or black here, I will let the market tell me what it wants to do. Above today's high of 7550 will mean the party is back on. Below 7290 and we are in for more chop, pullback. 
- Shifting to the QQQ which is where most of the high growth, high beta, high liquidity, exciting names reside. And that is not looking pretty. At point of writing, it is resting on the 50SMA and maybe the long weekend will be a good circuit breaker to shake everyone out of the current funk. 
- But let's turn to what I was leading to in this post, sectoral themes.
- Yes, the market still has 2 hours to go to close and it could all rally back in the next 2 hours. BUT, what I have been saying for a while is this: WIDE AND LOOSE is not good for the market environment. It implies indecision for many market players. 
- Let me go even more granular to illustrate what kind of "Technical Analysis" I normally use. Remember when $MU reported gang buster earnings? See what WDC did (more details in the prior post). It gapped up in sympathy but simply could not hold it. And proceeded to sell off. That was the first canary in the coal mine. And one by one, they all started FAILING TO HOLD GROUND. That was the 2nd sign. And today, we have THE market leader $SNDK coming off by 16% and counting. And remember the posts warning that Photonics was coming off and that they were the big "supply chain chokepoint" leaders but losing it? All these hinted to me that the AI CAPEX HARDWARE play was weakening.
- I've been a big believer and proponent of the AI CAPEX buildout bull story. I still do. But I rarely allow my own opinions to override what the market is telling me, and I NEVER allow it to override my risk management. The portfolio was essentially flat in June, avoiding the small loss in the indices, but more importantly, when the market was giving back even more, we didn't. And as we start the new month, recording a small loss but I think still better than what the market is doing. The main factor was getting out of the AI Hardware trade early enough, and rotating into the new leaders (cybersecurity and biotech) early enough. Software has been a mixed bag.
- Most people think trading is about finding the best setup, the best stock to trade or risk management. Truthfully, that is only the surface / level 1 stuff. What is often not talked about is WHEN to do more. WHEN to do less. WHEN to buy more, when less. When to even DO NOTHING! Not many people talk about that. If you follow me long enough, you will notice I don't necessarily make the most money. But I aim to lose very little money when the market is not conducive, but make a decent amount when the environment is good. This will allow us to have a smoother equity curve that is constantly sloping upwards, albeit with some small dips from time to time. 
- What I DON'T WANT to do is just follow the market up and down and give back all the profits we made so far this year. GIVING BACK prior profits is often what catches new traders off guard as they are still not used to treating the stock market as WAVES. There will be constant ebb and flow. Catch the flow, but don't give back too much during the ebb.
- All the best.

We are now experiencing another tech/high beta sell off similar to what we saw in November and February (both after big runs, this is no different)

Every sector has gotten hit lately: space, semiconductors, photonics, and neoclouds

I expect one final touch of the lower trendline in the channel.

Target would be $710-715

text:
Yeah, SemiAnalysis dropped a bearish photonics note on June 9, and about 3 weeks later they co-launched an ETF that holds $LITE. Hard to say it’s not intentional.

This is why I’d rather build conviction from my own work than trust a note that an analysis company posted. They act in their own interests.

text:
UDN Money report "殺翻光通訊後 SemiAnalysis 惹議 業界揭其劣跡 許多公司慘遭魔手":

- SemiAnalysis, released a report in June, questioning the timing of the launch of the CPO on the grounds of low yield and delay, which caused the photonics sector to collapse. 

- The agency then partnered with Tema to launch a photonics ETF. 

- The shares held in the ETF under the trading code LAZR happened to be the photonics stocks that were mentioned negatively in the report

$HIMX and $LITE, which were specifically targeted negatively in their article "Powered Down, Lights Off", have been bought in Semianalysis and Tema's new ETF after the optical sector crash.

This wasn’t highlighted in the original press release.

text:
blog's up 👇

short version - chips stopped shrinking, so the money moved from making silicon to packaging it. in silicon photonics, packaging is ~60-70% of the chip.

india spent $18bn on semis and spent almost all of it in commodity OSAT - the low margin part. the high-value, advanced packaging + photonics - has no listed indian pure-play in India.

except one - a small auto-software company from bangalore claiming india's only photonics packaging line - the tech story is genuinely interesting considering how they have build the capablity but the governance file is kinda scary.

not a reco. just us following the value chain.

https://t.co/PTZxygiWPR
embedded_text:
we've been reading a lot about advanced packaging (thanks to citrini year's start blog) and how we can play it via Indian players. 

we are pretty much stuck at OSAT on ground level given we have been a little* behind in terms of getting those capablities (a little😅)

posting on https://t.co/uIA67hWpAY

text:
#IZMO 
Silicon Photonics (SiPh) represents a massive fundamental shift in how data moves, driven by the structural bottlenecks of artificial intelligence workloads.

1. The Silicon Photonics Market: Why the Indirection Matters
Historically, microchips communicated internally and externally using electrons traveling over copper wires. However, as high-performance GenAI training clusters, large language models (LLMs), and hyperscale data centers expand, copper has hit a physical brick wall known as RC delay and skin effect. At extreme speeds, copper wires generate unsustainable heat, consume excessive power, and suffer severe signal degradation.

Silicon Photonics replaces electrons with photons (light). By fabricating optical waveguides directly onto silicon substrates, chips can transmit data using lasers.

Main Market Verticals (2026 Trajectory)
AI Clusters & Datacom: The primary growth engine. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) fuse optical engines right next to the compute ASIC/GPU, expanding transceiver bandwidth past 1.6T and 3.2T.

Aerospace & Defense: High-frequency Electronic Warfare (EW), phased-array radar signal processing, and space payloads where weight reduction and immunity to electromagnetic interference (EMI) are critical.

Quantum Computing: Distributing entangled photons across processing nodes via low-loss optical interconnects.

Major Indian Competitors 
India’s domestic chip landscape is heavily skewed toward legacy assembly, testing, and marking (ATMP) or standard wire-bonded packaging. True Silicon Photonics capability is highly restricted

IZMO’s Specific Role in the Value Chain: The Packaging Moat
In Silicon Photonics, packaging is the ultimate commercial bottleneck, routinely accounting for 60% to 80% of the total component cost.

While aligning electrical microbumps allows for minor margins of error, aligning an optical fiber to a silicon chip's internal sub-micron waveguide requires nanometer-scale precision. A misalignment of even a few hundred nanometers can cause catastrophic insertion loss, rendering the optical link useless.

Through its subsidiary, izmomicro (based in Whitefield, Bengaluru), Izmo has deliberately chosen not to compete in the high-cap-ex, low-margin foundry or standard assembly market. Instead, it has positioned itself as a specialized, high-barrier Advanced Packaging Partner.

text:
The Bottleneck In AI Just Met Its Match

As AI continues to scale, the conversation is shifting beyond compute to the infrastructure that powers it.

We're proud to see Neural I/o™ featured in @TheRundownAI, highlighting how Kopin and FabricAI are advancing MicroLED-based optical interconnect technology designed to address the bandwidth, latency, and power challenges of next-generation AI infrastructure.

Explore the feature to learn more and register to access our upcoming Neural I/o™ White Paper, where we take a deeper dive into the future of AI connectivity.

Learn more & register: https://t.co/V7yGqbR3hx

#KopinTech #ArtificialIntelligence #AIInfrastructure #MicroLED #Photonics #NeuralIO @FabricAIFABC @MiTknlg

text:
Relax. Touch grass. Go find some extra cash.

$META isn’t waving the white flag on AI. They’re building a cloud business to monetize excess AI compute capacity. This is exactly what you do when your infrastructure investments are working and you have more power than you need internally right now. It’s a feature, not a bug. The market just spun it as oversupply panic.

On the $NVDA side, the comments around Kyber rack delays and some architecture tweaks are real noise. But Nvidia’s core roadmap stays intact. Rubin moves into broader availability in the second half of this year. Ultra and the following platform come in 2027-2028. No serious competitor catches up in high-performance AI accelerators before then. Every major model provider and hyperscaler is trying to build their own custom chips, but those won’t be available at meaningful scale until 2028. Custom silicon from the big clouds remains complementary for now.

Memory chips (especially HBM): AI data centers are still on track to swallow around 70 percent of global memory production in 2026. HBM is sold out through the year across $SKHY, Samsung, and $MU. This is a genuine supercycle.
AI data center buildout keeps accelerating. Hyperscalers are guiding for hundreds of billions in capex this year. The physical infrastructure demand for power, cooling, and space is only increasing.

Any increased focus on open models could ultimately prove very positive for the industry. Inference workloads from on-demand open models will drive demand for efficient, low-cost compute at specialized data centers. Providers like $NBIS and $IREN are well positioned here because they deliver cheap power and purpose-built infrastructure that these models will depend on. 

As noted on the All-In podcast, David Friedberg has highlighted that AI cloud services could host the majority of workloads (around 70 percent in his view), while local hosting persists and is not going away, especially with open models running on demand.

Photonics demand grows with the need for better efficiency. Co-packaged optics and silicon photonics solve power and bandwidth limits inside and across racks. Even with some near-term rack timeline shifts, the structural requirement for optical interconnects rises as clusters get larger.

Existing connected and powered capacity remains valuable. Volume ramps may shift later than some models assumed, but that just gives more time to accumulate quality names in memory, data center infrastructure, and photonics-related plays.

Hyperscaler earnings are coming up soon. Capex commentary will tell the real story. The music is still playing?

text:
Silver Mesh, Photonics and Datacenters
Last night's dream was about selecting the best candidate to work with another. The best candidate was what appeared to be silver in a mesh configuration. But there were other possibilities as displayed in different colors. Enter light. The silver was also specifically displayed in generations. 
So, what's the application (at least so far)? 
"Here's a visualization of a hybrid silver mesh + photonics network in a datacenter context (e.g., AI server infrastructure)." -Grok
"In short, silver mesh + photonics hybrids are a promising direction for next-generation datacenter components. They align with trends in silicon photonics, plasmonics, and hybrid electro-optic integration, potentially allowing even more efficient use of silver while delivering superior speed, power efficiency, and functionality. Research in transparent conductors, plasmonic modulators, and hybrid packaging continues to advance these concepts." 
To help understand the magnitude of silver usage in datacenters, I'll put a link in the🧵.
